built with pdoc

psu_capstone.agent_layer

Public exports for the agent layer.

This package-level module exists to reduce import boilerplate in callers. Consumers can import the layer once, for example import psu_capstone.agent_layer as ag, and then reference ag.Brain or ag.Trainer instead of importing each symbol from its concrete module.

The exports are loaded lazily to avoid import-time side effects. In particular, Trainer pulls in plotting helpers and other heavier dependencies, so eager imports here would make lightweight package imports unexpectedly expensive and brittle.

 1"""Public exports for the agent layer.
 2
 3This package-level module exists to reduce import boilerplate in callers.
 4Consumers can import the layer once, for example import psu_capstone.agent_layer as ag,
 5and then reference ag.Brain or ag.Trainer instead of importing each symbol from
 6its concrete module.
 7
 8The exports are loaded lazily to avoid import-time side effects. In particular,
 9Trainer pulls in plotting helpers and other heavier dependencies, so eager imports
10here would make lightweight package imports unexpectedly expensive and brittle.
11"""
12
13from importlib import import_module
14from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
15
16HTM_MODULE = "psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM"
17
18if TYPE_CHECKING:
19    from psu_capstone.agent_layer.agent import Agent
20    from psu_capstone.agent_layer.agent_interface import AgentInterface
21    from psu_capstone.agent_layer.brain import Brain
22    from psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM import ColumnField, Field, InputField, OutputField
23    from psu_capstone.agent_layer.train import Trainer
24
25__all__ = [
26    "Agent",
27    "AgentInterface",
28    "Brain",
29    "ColumnField",
30    "Field",
31    "InputField",
32    "OutputField",
33    "Trainer",
34]
35
36_EXPORTS = {
37    "Agent": ("psu_capstone.agent_layer.agent", "Agent"),
38    "AgentInterface": ("psu_capstone.agent_layer.agent_interface", "AgentInterface"),
39    "Brain": ("psu_capstone.agent_layer.brain", "Brain"),
40    "ColumnField": (HTM_MODULE, "ColumnField"),
41    "Field": (HTM_MODULE, "Field"),
42    "InputField": (HTM_MODULE, "InputField"),
43    "OutputField": (HTM_MODULE, "OutputField"),
44    "Trainer": ("psu_capstone.agent_layer.train", "Trainer"),
45}
46
47
48def __getattr__(name: str) -> Any:
49    """Resolve layer exports lazily to keep package imports lightweight.
50
51    This preserves the convenient ag.Symbol API introduced by the import-cleanup
52    refactor while avoiding eager imports of modules that have deeper dependency trees.
53    """
54    if name not in _EXPORTS:
55        raise AttributeError(f"module {__name__!r} has no attribute {name!r}")
56    module_name, attr_name = _EXPORTS[name]
57    value = getattr(import_module(module_name), attr_name)
58    globals()[name] = value
59    return value
class Agent: 
 29class Agent:
 30    """Coordinate Brain inference, policy selection, and environment interaction.
 31
 32    The Agent is the runtime owner of the RL loop. On each timestep it:
 33
 34    1. reads the current observation cached from the environment
 35    2. feeds the flattened observation inputs into the Brain
 36    3. selects an action using the active policy mode
 37    4. executes that action through the environment adapter
 38    5. stores the resulting next observation for the following timestep
 39
 40    The Agent supports multiple action-selection strategies through
 41    ``policy_mode``. At the moment those are:
 42
 43    - ``q_table``: epsilon-greedy action selection over a tabular value store
 44    - ``ppo``: action selection delegated to an injected PPO-style model
 45    - ``brain``: prefer Brain predictions when usable, otherwise fall back to
 46      the Q-table selector
 47
 48    Args:
 49        brain: HTM Brain instance that consumes flattened observation inputs
 50            and produces predictions.
 51        adapter: Environment adapter that bridges Gym-style spaces and raw
 52            values into forms usable by the Agent and Brain.
 53        episodes: Number of training episodes to run when ``train`` is called.
 54        policy_mode: Active action-selection strategy. ``q_table`` uses
 55            epsilon-greedy lookup over cached values. ``ppo`` delegates action
 56            selection to a PPO-like model. ``brain`` attempts to rank actions
 57            using Brain predictions before falling back.
 58        ppo_policy: Stable-Baselines3 policy class name used when creating
 59            a PPO model internally.
 60        ppo_kwargs: Optional keyword arguments forwarded to
 61            ``stable_baselines3.PPO`` during model construction.
 62        ppo_deterministic: Passed to ``ppo_model.predict`` when policy mode is
 63            ``ppo``.
 64
 65    Raises:
 66        TypeError: If ``policy_mode`` is ``'q_table'`` but the environment
 67            does not have a Discrete action space.
 68    """
 69
 70    def __init__(
 71        self,
 72        brain: Brain,
 73        adapter: EnvAdapter,
 74        episodes: int = 1000,
 75        policy_mode: Literal["q_table", "brain", "ppo"] = "ppo",
 76        ppo_policy: str = "MlpPolicy",
 77        ppo_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
 78        ppo_deterministic: bool = True,
 79        force_brain_mode: bool = False,
 80    ) -> None:
 81        self._learning_rate: float = 0.1
 82        self._discount_factor: float = 0.99
 83        self._epsilon: float = 1.0
 84        self._epsilon_decay: float = 0.01
 85
 86        self._brain = brain
 87        self._adapter = adapter
 88        self._logger = get_logger(self)
 89        self._policy_mode: Literal["q_table", "brain", "ppo"] = policy_mode
 90        self._ppo_policy = ppo_policy
 91        self._ppo_kwargs = ppo_kwargs or {}
 92        self._ppo_model: PPO | None = None
 93        self._ppo_deterministic = ppo_deterministic
 94        self._brain_action_confidence_threshold: float = 0.1
 95        self._force_brain_mode: bool = force_brain_mode
 96        self._q_state_decimals: int = 2
 97
 98        # q_table policy only makes sense when actions can be indexed.
 99        action_spec = self._adapter.get_action_spec()
100        action_count = action_spec.get("n")
101        is_integer_like = isinstance(action_count, (int, np.integer))
102        if self._policy_mode == "q_table" and (
103            action_spec.get("space_type") != "Discrete" or not is_integer_like
104        ):
105            raise TypeError("q_table policy requires a Discrete action space.")
106
107        self._action_count: int | None = int(action_count) if is_integer_like else None
108        self._q_values: defaultdict[Any, np.ndarray] = defaultdict(self._new_q_row)
109
110        self._training_episodes: int = episodes
111        self._training_error: list[float] = []
112        self._obs: Any | None = None
113        self._inputs: dict[str, Any] = {}
114        self._rng = random.Random()
115        self._last_action_source: str = "unknown"
116        self._last_reward: float = 0.0
117
118        if self._policy_mode == "ppo":
119            self._ppo_model = self._build_ppo_model()
120
121    def _build_ppo_model(self) -> PPO:
122        """Create a Stable-Baselines3 PPO model bound to the adapter environment."""
123
124        if not hasattr(self._adapter, "_env"):
125            raise ValueError("ppo policy mode requires an adapter with a Gym environment (_env).")
126
127        env = self._adapter._env
128        kwargs = {"device": "cpu", **self._ppo_kwargs}
129        return PPO(self._ppo_policy, env, verbose=0, **kwargs)
130
131    def _select_ppo_action(self, obs: Any) -> Any:
132        """Select an action from the configured PPO-style policy model."""
133
134        if self._ppo_model is None:
135            self._ppo_model = self._build_ppo_model()
136
137        action, _state = self._ppo_model.predict(obs, deterministic=self._ppo_deterministic)
138        self._last_action_source = "ppo"
139        if isinstance(action, np.ndarray) and action.shape == ():
140            return action.item()
141        return action
142
143    def train_ppo(self, total_timesteps: int = 50_000) -> None:
144        """Train the PPO model for the given number of environment timesteps.
145
146        Args:
147            total_timesteps: Total environment steps to train for.
148                50 000 is enough for CartPole-v1 to converge reliably.
149        """
150        if self._policy_mode != "ppo":
151            raise RuntimeError("train_ppo() called but policy_mode is not 'ppo'.")
152        if self._ppo_model is None:
153            self._ppo_model = self._build_ppo_model()
154        self._logger.info("Training PPO for %d timesteps...", total_timesteps)
155        self._ppo_model.learn(total_timesteps=total_timesteps)
156        self._logger.info("PPO training complete.")
157
158    def switch_policy(self, new_mode: Literal["q_table", "brain", "ppo"]) -> None:
159        """Switch the active policy mode at runtime.
160
161        Args:
162            new_mode: The policy mode to switch to.
163        """
164        self._logger.info("Switching policy from '%s' to '%s'.", self._policy_mode, new_mode)
165        self._policy_mode = new_mode
166
167    def _new_q_row(self) -> np.ndarray:
168        """Create a zero-initialized action-value row for one state."""
169
170        if self._action_count is None:
171            return np.array([], dtype=float)
172
173        return np.zeros(self._action_count, dtype=float)
174
175    def _state_key(self, obs: Any) -> tuple[tuple[str, Any], ...]:
176        """Convert raw observations into a hashable key for tabular lookup."""
177
178        # Use adapter-normalized inputs so state identity matches Brain-facing features.
179        obs_inputs = self._adapter.observation_to_inputs(obs)
180        quantized_inputs = {
181            key: (
182                round(float(value), self._q_state_decimals)
183                if isinstance(value, (int, float, np.integer, np.floating))
184                else value
185            )
186            for key, value in obs_inputs.items()
187        }
188        return tuple(sorted(quantized_inputs.items()))
189
190    def reset_episode(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
191        """Reset the environment, then cache the initial observation.
192
193        Returns:
194            Adapter reset bridge containing at least the raw observation,
195            flattened HTM inputs, and environment info.
196        """
197
198        result = self._adapter.reset_bridge()
199        self._obs = result["obs"]
200        self._inputs = result["inputs"]
201        self._last_reward = 0.0
202        return result
203
204    def _initialize_q_values(self, obs: Any) -> tuple[tuple[str, Any], ...]:
205        """Ensure an observation has an entry in the tabular value store."""
206
207        state_key = self._state_key(obs)
208
209        # Touching the defaultdict lazily allocates a zero row for unseen states.
210        _ = self._q_values[state_key]
211        return state_key
212
213    def _action_index(self, action: Any) -> int | None:
214        """Resolve an action value to its tabular Q-row index when possible."""
215
216        if isinstance(action, (int, np.integer)) and self._action_count is not None:
217            index = int(action)
218            if 0 <= index < self._action_count:
219                return index
220
221        candidate_actions = self._candidate_actions()
222        if not candidate_actions:
223            return None
224
225        for index, candidate in enumerate(candidate_actions):
226            if candidate == action:
227                return index
228
229        return None
230
231    def _candidate_actions(self) -> list[Any]:
232        """Enumerate actions when the adapter exposes a finite action list.
233
234        Returns:
235            A flat list of candidate environment actions for action spaces that
236            can be enumerated directly. Returns an empty list for spaces that
237            are continuous or otherwise not represented as a single flat list of
238            values.
239        """
240
241        action_spec = self._adapter.get_action_spec()
242        values = action_spec.get("values")
243
244        if isinstance(values, list) and all(not isinstance(item, list) for item in values):
245            return values
246
247        return []
248
249    def _select_q_action(self, obs: Any) -> Any:
250        """Select an action using epsilon-greedy Q-table behavior.
251
252        This strategy prefers exploration while ``epsilon`` is high, then
253        gradually shifts toward exploitation of the best known action value for
254        the current observation.
255
256        Args:
257            obs: Current environment observation.
258
259        Returns:
260            A concrete action compatible with the environment action space.
261        """
262
263        state_key = self._initialize_q_values(obs)
264
265        if self._epsilon > 0.1:
266            self._epsilon -= self._epsilon_decay
267
268        candidate_actions = self._candidate_actions()
269
270        if not candidate_actions:
271            self._last_action_source = "q_table"
272            return self._adapter._action_space.sample()
273
274        # Epsilon-greedy action selection
275        if self._rng.random() < self._epsilon:
276            self._last_action_source = "q_table"
277            return self._adapter._action_space.sample()
278
279        q_row = self._q_values[state_key]
280
281        # If spec/value mismatch occurs, degrade safely to random exploration.
282        if len(q_row) != len(candidate_actions):
283            self._last_action_source = "q_table"
284            return self._adapter._action_space.sample()
285
286        max_q = float(np.max(q_row))
287        best_indices = [index for index, value in enumerate(q_row) if float(value) == max_q]
288        best_index = self._rng.choice(best_indices)
289        self._last_action_source = "q_table"
290        return candidate_actions[best_index]
291
292    def _score_predicted_action(
293        self,
294        action_inputs: dict[str, Any],
295        predictions: dict[str, Any],
296    ) -> float:
297        """Score how well a candidate action aligns with Brain predictions.
298
299        Numeric predictions are scored by distance, optionally weighted by a
300        ``<field>.conf`` confidence value. Non-numeric predictions receive a
301        positive score when they match exactly.
302
303        Args:
304            action_inputs: Flattened representation of one candidate action.
305            predictions: Brain prediction mapping, including optional
306                confidence entries.
307
308        Returns:
309            A larger score for better alignment with the predicted action-like
310            outputs.
311        """
312
313        score = 0.0
314
315        for key, value in action_inputs.items():
316            if key not in predictions or predictions[key] is None:
317                continue
318
319            prediction = predictions[key]
320            confidence = predictions.get(f"{key}.conf", 1.0)
321            weight = float(confidence) if isinstance(confidence, (int, float)) else 1.0
322
323            if isinstance(prediction, (int, float)) and isinstance(value, (int, float)):
324                score -= abs(float(prediction) - float(value)) * weight
325            else:
326                score += weight if prediction == value else 0.0
327
328        return score
329
330    def _select_brain_action(self, obs: Any) -> Any:
331        """Select an action using Brain predictions when available.
332
333        This strategy asks the Brain for predictions, filters for fields that
334        look like action outputs, and ranks candidate actions against those
335        predictions. If the Brain does not expose usable action predictions,
336        selection falls back to the Q-table policy.
337
338        Args:
339            obs: Current environment observation.
340
341        Returns:
342            A concrete environment action.
343        """
344
345        return self._select_brain_action_from_outputs(obs, brain_outputs=None)
346
347    def _extract_action_from_brain_outputs(
348        self, brain_outputs: dict[str, Any]
349    ) -> tuple[Any | None, float]:
350        """Extract direct action hint and confidence from Brain.step output payloads."""
351
352        for _name, payload in brain_outputs.items():
353            if isinstance(payload, dict) and "action" in payload:
354                confidence = payload.get("confidence", 1.0)
355                confidence_value = (
356                    float(confidence) if isinstance(confidence, (int, float)) else 1.0
357                )
358                return payload["action"], confidence_value
359
360        return None, 0.0
361
362    def _select_brain_action_from_outputs(
363        self,
364        obs: Any,
365        brain_outputs: dict[str, Any] | None,
366    ) -> Any:
367        """Select action from output-field hints, then prediction scoring.
368
369        Fallback chain: brain → ppo → q_table.
370        """
371
372        if isinstance(brain_outputs, dict):
373            action_hint, confidence = self._extract_action_from_brain_outputs(brain_outputs)
374            if action_hint is not None and (
375                confidence >= self._brain_action_confidence_threshold or self._force_brain_mode
376            ):
377                self._last_action_source = "brain"
378                if self._force_brain_mode and confidence < self._brain_action_confidence_threshold:
379                    self._logger.info(
380                        "force_brain_mode: using brain action with low confidence %.3f.",
381                        confidence,
382                    )
383                return action_hint
384            if action_hint is not None:
385                self._logger.info(
386                    "Brain action confidence %.3f below threshold %.3f; falling back to ppo.",
387                    confidence,
388                    self._brain_action_confidence_threshold,
389                )
390                return self._select_ppo_or_q_action(obs)
391
392        candidate_actions = self._candidate_actions()
393
394        if not candidate_actions:
395            self._last_action_source = "brain"
396            return self._adapter._action_space.sample()
397
398        predictions = self._brain.prediction()
399        if not isinstance(predictions, dict):
400            self._logger.info("Brain predictions unavailable; falling back to ppo.")
401            return self._select_ppo_or_q_action(obs)
402
403        action_predictions = {
404            key: value for key, value in predictions.items() if key.startswith("action")
405        }
406
407        if not action_predictions:
408            self._logger.info("No action-like brain predictions; falling back to ppo.")
409            return self._select_ppo_or_q_action(obs)
410
411        action = max(
412            candidate_actions,
413            key=lambda action: self._score_predicted_action(
414                self._adapter.action_to_inputs(action),
415                action_predictions,
416            ),
417        )
418        self._last_action_source = "brain"
419        return action
420
421    def _select_ppo_or_q_action(self, obs: Any) -> Any:
422        """Use PPO first, then q-table as the final safety net.
423
424        This helper is used when brain mode cannot confidently choose an action.
425        If PPO cannot produce an action (for example, model init issues), we
426        still return a valid action by delegating to q-table selection.
427        """
428        try:
429            return self._select_ppo_action(obs)
430        except Exception:
431            self._logger.info("PPO unavailable; falling back to q_table.")
432            return self._select_q_action(obs)
433
434    def select_action(self, obs: Any, brain_outputs: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> Any:
435        """Dispatch action selection to the active policy implementation.
436
437        Args:
438            obs: Current environment observation.
439            brain_outputs: Optional dict of Brain prediction outputs used when
440                ``policy_mode`` is ``'brain'``.
441
442        Returns:
443            The environment action chosen by the active policy mode.
444
445        Notes:
446            - ``mode`` is the configured top-level policy.
447            - ``source`` (logged each step) is the component that actually
448              produced the action after fallback logic.
449              Example: mode=brain, source=ppo.
450        """
451
452        if self._policy_mode == "brain":
453            action = self._select_brain_action_from_outputs(obs, brain_outputs)
454        elif self._policy_mode == "ppo":
455            action = self._select_ppo_action(obs)
456        else:
457            action = self._select_q_action(obs)
458
459        self._logger.info(
460            "Policy step: mode=%s source=%s action=%s",
461            self._policy_mode,
462            self._last_action_source,
463            action,
464        )
465        return action
466
467    def step(self, learn: bool = True) -> dict[str, Any]:
468        """Run one complete agent-controlled environment timestep.
469
470        The current cached observation is first converted into Brain inputs and
471        processed by ``Brain.step``. The Agent then selects an environment
472        action, applies it through the adapter, receives the resulting
473        transition, and caches the next observation for the following call.
474
475        Args:
476            learn: Passed through to ``Brain.step`` to enable or disable Brain
477                learning for this timestep.
478
479        Returns:
480            A transition dictionary containing the current observation and
481            inputs, chosen action, next observation and inputs, reward, done
482            flags, and environment info.
483
484        Raises:
485            RuntimeError: If no observation is available after attempting to
486                initialize the episode state.
487        """
488
489        if self._obs is None:
490            # First step in an episode initializes cached observation/input state.
491            self.reset_episode()
492
493        current_obs = self._obs
494        current_inputs = self._inputs
495
496        if current_obs is None:
497            raise RuntimeError("Agent observation state was not initialized.")
498
499        step_inputs = dict(current_inputs)
500        step_inputs.setdefault("reward", float(self._last_reward))
501
502        brain_outputs = self._brain.step(step_inputs, learn=learn)
503        action = self.select_action(current_obs, brain_outputs=brain_outputs)
504        result = self._adapter.step_bridge(action)
505
506        next_obs = result["obs"]
507        reward = result["reward"]
508        self._last_reward = float(reward)
509
510        self.update(
511            current_obs,
512            action,
513            reward,
514            next_obs,
515            done=result["terminated"] or result["truncated"],
516        )
517
518        # Cache the next timestep state so the next Agent.step() can continue the loop.
519        self._obs = next_obs
520        self._inputs = result["inputs"]
521
522        return {
523            "obs": current_obs,
524            "inputs": current_inputs,
525            "action": action,
526            "next_obs": next_obs,
527            "next_inputs": result["inputs"],
528            "reward": reward,
529            "terminated": result["terminated"],
530            "truncated": result["truncated"],
531            "info": result["info"],
532        }
533
534    def update(
535        self,
536        obs: Any,
537        action: Any,
538        reward: float,
539        next_obs: Any,
540        done: bool = False,
541    ) -> None:
542        """Update policy state from a transition.
543
544                Plain-language policy update rules:
545
546                - ``q_table``: apply one-step TD update.
547                - ``ppo``: no update here; PPO training is managed separately.
548                - ``brain``: keep the current RL update stub in place. If this step
549                    actually used q-table fallback, run the q-table TD update so those
550                    fallback actions can still learn tabular values.
551
552        Args:
553            obs: Observation before the action was taken.
554            action: Environment action selected for the timestep.
555            reward: Reward returned by the environment.
556            next_obs: Observation returned after the transition.
557            done: Whether the transition ended the episode.
558        """
559
560        if self._policy_mode == "ppo":
561            # PPO model training lifecycle is managed externally.
562            return
563
564        if self._policy_mode == "brain":
565            if self._last_action_source == "q_table":
566                # In brain mode, allow tabular updates when action selection
567                # explicitly fell back to q_table behavior.
568                pass
569            else:
570                # Brain RL update is intentionally a stub for now; we keep this
571                # explicit so a future reward-driven Brain update can drop in.
572                self._brain.rl_policy_update()
573                return
574
575        if self._action_count is None:
576            return
577
578        action_index = self._action_index(action)
579        if action_index is None:
580            return
581
582        state_key = self._initialize_q_values(obs)
583        next_state_key = self._initialize_q_values(next_obs)
584
585        current_q = float(self._q_values[state_key][action_index])
586        next_best_q = 0.0 if done else float(np.max(self._q_values[next_state_key]))
587
588        td_target = float(reward) + (self._discount_factor * next_best_q)
589        td_error = td_target - current_q
590
591        self._q_values[state_key][action_index] = current_q + (self._learning_rate * td_error)
592        self._training_error.append(abs(td_error))
593
594    def train(self) -> None:
595        """Run the configured number of episodes using agent-owned stepping.
596
597        Each episode is reset through ``reset_episode`` and advanced by calling
598        ``step`` until the environment reports termination or truncation.
599        """
600
601        for _episode in range(self._training_episodes):
602            self.reset_episode()
603            done = False
604
605            while not done:
606                transition = self.step(learn=True)
607                done = transition["terminated"] or transition["truncated"]

Coordinate Brain inference, policy selection, and environment interaction.

The Agent is the runtime owner of the RL loop. On each timestep it:

  1. reads the current observation cached from the environment
  2. feeds the flattened observation inputs into the Brain
  3. selects an action using the active policy mode
  4. executes that action through the environment adapter
  5. stores the resulting next observation for the following timestep

The Agent supports multiple action-selection strategies through policy_mode. At the moment those are:

  • q_table: epsilon-greedy action selection over a tabular value store
  • ppo: action selection delegated to an injected PPO-style model
  • brain: prefer Brain predictions when usable, otherwise fall back to the Q-table selector

Args: brain: HTM Brain instance that consumes flattened observation inputs and produces predictions. adapter: Environment adapter that bridges Gym-style spaces and raw values into forms usable by the Agent and Brain. episodes: Number of training episodes to run when train is called. policy_mode: Active action-selection strategy. q_table uses epsilon-greedy lookup over cached values. ppo delegates action selection to a PPO-like model. brain attempts to rank actions using Brain predictions before falling back. ppo_policy: Stable-Baselines3 policy class name used when creating a PPO model internally. ppo_kwargs: Optional keyword arguments forwarded to stable_baselines3.PPO during model construction. ppo_deterministic: Passed to ppo_model.predict when policy mode is ppo.

Raises: TypeError: If policy_mode is 'q_table' but the environment does not have a Discrete action space.

Agent( brain: Brain, adapter: psu_capstone.environment.EnvAdapter, episodes: int = 1000, policy_mode: Literal['q_table', 'brain', 'ppo'] = 'ppo', ppo_policy: str = 'MlpPolicy', ppo_kwargs: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None, ppo_deterministic: bool = True, force_brain_mode: bool = False) 
 70    def __init__(
 71        self,
 72        brain: Brain,
 73        adapter: EnvAdapter,
 74        episodes: int = 1000,
 75        policy_mode: Literal["q_table", "brain", "ppo"] = "ppo",
 76        ppo_policy: str = "MlpPolicy",
 77        ppo_kwargs: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
 78        ppo_deterministic: bool = True,
 79        force_brain_mode: bool = False,
 80    ) -> None:
 81        self._learning_rate: float = 0.1
 82        self._discount_factor: float = 0.99
 83        self._epsilon: float = 1.0
 84        self._epsilon_decay: float = 0.01
 85
 86        self._brain = brain
 87        self._adapter = adapter
 88        self._logger = get_logger(self)
 89        self._policy_mode: Literal["q_table", "brain", "ppo"] = policy_mode
 90        self._ppo_policy = ppo_policy
 91        self._ppo_kwargs = ppo_kwargs or {}
 92        self._ppo_model: PPO | None = None
 93        self._ppo_deterministic = ppo_deterministic
 94        self._brain_action_confidence_threshold: float = 0.1
 95        self._force_brain_mode: bool = force_brain_mode
 96        self._q_state_decimals: int = 2
 97
 98        # q_table policy only makes sense when actions can be indexed.
 99        action_spec = self._adapter.get_action_spec()
100        action_count = action_spec.get("n")
101        is_integer_like = isinstance(action_count, (int, np.integer))
102        if self._policy_mode == "q_table" and (
103            action_spec.get("space_type") != "Discrete" or not is_integer_like
104        ):
105            raise TypeError("q_table policy requires a Discrete action space.")
106
107        self._action_count: int | None = int(action_count) if is_integer_like else None
108        self._q_values: defaultdict[Any, np.ndarray] = defaultdict(self._new_q_row)
109
110        self._training_episodes: int = episodes
111        self._training_error: list[float] = []
112        self._obs: Any | None = None
113        self._inputs: dict[str, Any] = {}
114        self._rng = random.Random()
115        self._last_action_source: str = "unknown"
116        self._last_reward: float = 0.0
117
118        if self._policy_mode == "ppo":
119            self._ppo_model = self._build_ppo_model()
def train_ppo(self, total_timesteps: int = 50000) -> None: 
143    def train_ppo(self, total_timesteps: int = 50_000) -> None:
144        """Train the PPO model for the given number of environment timesteps.
145
146        Args:
147            total_timesteps: Total environment steps to train for.
148                50 000 is enough for CartPole-v1 to converge reliably.
149        """
150        if self._policy_mode != "ppo":
151            raise RuntimeError("train_ppo() called but policy_mode is not 'ppo'.")
152        if self._ppo_model is None:
153            self._ppo_model = self._build_ppo_model()
154        self._logger.info("Training PPO for %d timesteps...", total_timesteps)
155        self._ppo_model.learn(total_timesteps=total_timesteps)
156        self._logger.info("PPO training complete.")

Train the PPO model for the given number of environment timesteps.

Args: total_timesteps: Total environment steps to train for. 50 000 is enough for CartPole-v1 to converge reliably.

def switch_policy(self, new_mode: Literal['q_table', 'brain', 'ppo']) -> None: 
158    def switch_policy(self, new_mode: Literal["q_table", "brain", "ppo"]) -> None:
159        """Switch the active policy mode at runtime.
160
161        Args:
162            new_mode: The policy mode to switch to.
163        """
164        self._logger.info("Switching policy from '%s' to '%s'.", self._policy_mode, new_mode)
165        self._policy_mode = new_mode

Switch the active policy mode at runtime.

Args: new_mode: The policy mode to switch to.

def reset_episode(self) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
190    def reset_episode(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
191        """Reset the environment, then cache the initial observation.
192
193        Returns:
194            Adapter reset bridge containing at least the raw observation,
195            flattened HTM inputs, and environment info.
196        """
197
198        result = self._adapter.reset_bridge()
199        self._obs = result["obs"]
200        self._inputs = result["inputs"]
201        self._last_reward = 0.0
202        return result

Reset the environment, then cache the initial observation.

Returns: Adapter reset bridge containing at least the raw observation, flattened HTM inputs, and environment info.

def select_action( self, obs: Any, brain_outputs: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None) -> Any: 
434    def select_action(self, obs: Any, brain_outputs: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> Any:
435        """Dispatch action selection to the active policy implementation.
436
437        Args:
438            obs: Current environment observation.
439            brain_outputs: Optional dict of Brain prediction outputs used when
440                ``policy_mode`` is ``'brain'``.
441
442        Returns:
443            The environment action chosen by the active policy mode.
444
445        Notes:
446            - ``mode`` is the configured top-level policy.
447            - ``source`` (logged each step) is the component that actually
448              produced the action after fallback logic.
449              Example: mode=brain, source=ppo.
450        """
451
452        if self._policy_mode == "brain":
453            action = self._select_brain_action_from_outputs(obs, brain_outputs)
454        elif self._policy_mode == "ppo":
455            action = self._select_ppo_action(obs)
456        else:
457            action = self._select_q_action(obs)
458
459        self._logger.info(
460            "Policy step: mode=%s source=%s action=%s",
461            self._policy_mode,
462            self._last_action_source,
463            action,
464        )
465        return action

Dispatch action selection to the active policy implementation.

Args: obs: Current environment observation. brain_outputs: Optional dict of Brain prediction outputs used when policy_mode is 'brain'.

Returns: The environment action chosen by the active policy mode.

Notes: - mode is the configured top-level policy. - source (logged each step) is the component that actually produced the action after fallback logic. Example: mode=brain, source=ppo.

def step(self, learn: bool = True) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
467    def step(self, learn: bool = True) -> dict[str, Any]:
468        """Run one complete agent-controlled environment timestep.
469
470        The current cached observation is first converted into Brain inputs and
471        processed by ``Brain.step``. The Agent then selects an environment
472        action, applies it through the adapter, receives the resulting
473        transition, and caches the next observation for the following call.
474
475        Args:
476            learn: Passed through to ``Brain.step`` to enable or disable Brain
477                learning for this timestep.
478
479        Returns:
480            A transition dictionary containing the current observation and
481            inputs, chosen action, next observation and inputs, reward, done
482            flags, and environment info.
483
484        Raises:
485            RuntimeError: If no observation is available after attempting to
486                initialize the episode state.
487        """
488
489        if self._obs is None:
490            # First step in an episode initializes cached observation/input state.
491            self.reset_episode()
492
493        current_obs = self._obs
494        current_inputs = self._inputs
495
496        if current_obs is None:
497            raise RuntimeError("Agent observation state was not initialized.")
498
499        step_inputs = dict(current_inputs)
500        step_inputs.setdefault("reward", float(self._last_reward))
501
502        brain_outputs = self._brain.step(step_inputs, learn=learn)
503        action = self.select_action(current_obs, brain_outputs=brain_outputs)
504        result = self._adapter.step_bridge(action)
505
506        next_obs = result["obs"]
507        reward = result["reward"]
508        self._last_reward = float(reward)
509
510        self.update(
511            current_obs,
512            action,
513            reward,
514            next_obs,
515            done=result["terminated"] or result["truncated"],
516        )
517
518        # Cache the next timestep state so the next Agent.step() can continue the loop.
519        self._obs = next_obs
520        self._inputs = result["inputs"]
521
522        return {
523            "obs": current_obs,
524            "inputs": current_inputs,
525            "action": action,
526            "next_obs": next_obs,
527            "next_inputs": result["inputs"],
528            "reward": reward,
529            "terminated": result["terminated"],
530            "truncated": result["truncated"],
531            "info": result["info"],
532        }

Run one complete agent-controlled environment timestep.

The current cached observation is first converted into Brain inputs and processed by Brain.step. The Agent then selects an environment action, applies it through the adapter, receives the resulting transition, and caches the next observation for the following call.

Args: learn: Passed through to Brain.step to enable or disable Brain learning for this timestep.

Returns: A transition dictionary containing the current observation and inputs, chosen action, next observation and inputs, reward, done flags, and environment info.

Raises: RuntimeError: If no observation is available after attempting to initialize the episode state.

def update( self, obs: Any, action: Any, reward: float, next_obs: Any, done: bool = False) -> None: 
534    def update(
535        self,
536        obs: Any,
537        action: Any,
538        reward: float,
539        next_obs: Any,
540        done: bool = False,
541    ) -> None:
542        """Update policy state from a transition.
543
544                Plain-language policy update rules:
545
546                - ``q_table``: apply one-step TD update.
547                - ``ppo``: no update here; PPO training is managed separately.
548                - ``brain``: keep the current RL update stub in place. If this step
549                    actually used q-table fallback, run the q-table TD update so those
550                    fallback actions can still learn tabular values.
551
552        Args:
553            obs: Observation before the action was taken.
554            action: Environment action selected for the timestep.
555            reward: Reward returned by the environment.
556            next_obs: Observation returned after the transition.
557            done: Whether the transition ended the episode.
558        """
559
560        if self._policy_mode == "ppo":
561            # PPO model training lifecycle is managed externally.
562            return
563
564        if self._policy_mode == "brain":
565            if self._last_action_source == "q_table":
566                # In brain mode, allow tabular updates when action selection
567                # explicitly fell back to q_table behavior.
568                pass
569            else:
570                # Brain RL update is intentionally a stub for now; we keep this
571                # explicit so a future reward-driven Brain update can drop in.
572                self._brain.rl_policy_update()
573                return
574
575        if self._action_count is None:
576            return
577
578        action_index = self._action_index(action)
579        if action_index is None:
580            return
581
582        state_key = self._initialize_q_values(obs)
583        next_state_key = self._initialize_q_values(next_obs)
584
585        current_q = float(self._q_values[state_key][action_index])
586        next_best_q = 0.0 if done else float(np.max(self._q_values[next_state_key]))
587
588        td_target = float(reward) + (self._discount_factor * next_best_q)
589        td_error = td_target - current_q
590
591        self._q_values[state_key][action_index] = current_q + (self._learning_rate * td_error)
592        self._training_error.append(abs(td_error))

Update policy state from a transition.

    Plain-language policy update rules:

    - ``q_table``: apply one-step TD update.
    - ``ppo``: no update here; PPO training is managed separately.
    - ``brain``: keep the current RL update stub in place. If this step
        actually used q-table fallback, run the q-table TD update so those
        fallback actions can still learn tabular values.

Args: obs: Observation before the action was taken. action: Environment action selected for the timestep. reward: Reward returned by the environment. next_obs: Observation returned after the transition. done: Whether the transition ended the episode.

def train(self) -> None: 
594    def train(self) -> None:
595        """Run the configured number of episodes using agent-owned stepping.
596
597        Each episode is reset through ``reset_episode`` and advanced by calling
598        ``step`` until the environment reports termination or truncation.
599        """
600
601        for _episode in range(self._training_episodes):
602            self.reset_episode()
603            done = False
604
605            while not done:
606                transition = self.step(learn=True)
607                done = transition["terminated"] or transition["truncated"]

Run the configured number of episodes using agent-owned stepping.

Each episode is reset through reset_episode and advanced by calling step until the environment reports termination or truncation.

@runtime_checkable
class AgentInterface(typing.Protocol): 
16@runtime_checkable
17class AgentInterface(Protocol):
18    """Protocol defining the interface requirements for HTM-RL agents.
19
20    Agents implementing this protocol can select actions based on state
21    observations and update their internal policy based on experience
22    in reinforcement learning environments.
23    """
24
25    def select_action(self, state: tuple) -> Any:
26        """Select an action based on the current state observation.
27
28        Processes the current state through the agent's decision-making
29        mechanism (typically HTM + policy network) to determine the best action.
30
31        Args:
32            state: Current environment state as a tuple of observations.
33
34        Returns:
35            The action to take in the environment.
36        """
37        ...
38
39    def step(self, action: Any) -> tuple[np.ndarray, float, bool, dict[str, Any]]:
40        """Execute the given action and return the resulting experience tuple.
41
42        This method should interact with the environment to perform the action,
43        observe the next state, receive the reward, and determine if the episode
44        has terminated.
45
46        Args:
47            action: The action to execute in the environment.
48
49        Returns:
50            A tuple of (next_state, reward, done, info) where:
51                - next_state: The new state observation after taking the action.
52                - reward: The reward received from the environment.
53                - done: A boolean indicating if the episode has ended.
54                - info: A dictionary with any additional information from the environment.
55        """
56
57        ...
58
59    def update(self, state: tuple, action: Any, reward: float, next_state: tuple) -> None:
60        """Update the agent's internal model and policy based on experience.
61
62        This method should use the experience tuple (state, action, reward, next_state)
63        to update the agent's learning mechanism, such as updating the HTM model
64        or training a policy network.
65
66        Args:
67            state: The state observed before taking the action.
68            action: The action taken by the agent.
69            reward: The reward received from the environment.
70            next_state: The state observed after taking the action.
71        """
72
73        ...

Protocol defining the interface requirements for HTM-RL agents.

Agents implementing this protocol can select actions based on state observations and update their internal policy based on experience in reinforcement learning environments.

AgentInterface(*args, **kwargs) 
1771def _no_init_or_replace_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
1772    cls = type(self)
1773
1774    if cls._is_protocol:
1775        raise TypeError('Protocols cannot be instantiated')
1776
1777    # Already using a custom `__init__`. No need to calculate correct
1778    # `__init__` to call. This can lead to RecursionError. See bpo-45121.
1779    if cls.__init__ is not _no_init_or_replace_init:
1780        return
1781
1782    # Initially, `__init__` of a protocol subclass is set to `_no_init_or_replace_init`.
1783    # The first instantiation of the subclass will call `_no_init_or_replace_init` which
1784    # searches for a proper new `__init__` in the MRO. The new `__init__`
1785    # replaces the subclass' old `__init__` (ie `_no_init_or_replace_init`). Subsequent
1786    # instantiation of the protocol subclass will thus use the new
1787    # `__init__` and no longer call `_no_init_or_replace_init`.
1788    for base in cls.__mro__:
1789        init = base.__dict__.get('__init__', _no_init_or_replace_init)
1790        if init is not _no_init_or_replace_init:
1791            cls.__init__ = init
1792            break
1793    else:
1794        # should not happen
1795        cls.__init__ = object.__init__
1796
1797    cls.__init__(self, *args, **kwargs)
def select_action(self, state: tuple) -> Any: 
25    def select_action(self, state: tuple) -> Any:
26        """Select an action based on the current state observation.
27
28        Processes the current state through the agent's decision-making
29        mechanism (typically HTM + policy network) to determine the best action.
30
31        Args:
32            state: Current environment state as a tuple of observations.
33
34        Returns:
35            The action to take in the environment.
36        """
37        ...

Select an action based on the current state observation.

Processes the current state through the agent's decision-making mechanism (typically HTM + policy network) to determine the best action.

Args: state: Current environment state as a tuple of observations.

Returns: The action to take in the environment.

def step( self, action: Any) -> tuple[numpy.ndarray, float, bool, dict[str, typing.Any]]: 
39    def step(self, action: Any) -> tuple[np.ndarray, float, bool, dict[str, Any]]:
40        """Execute the given action and return the resulting experience tuple.
41
42        This method should interact with the environment to perform the action,
43        observe the next state, receive the reward, and determine if the episode
44        has terminated.
45
46        Args:
47            action: The action to execute in the environment.
48
49        Returns:
50            A tuple of (next_state, reward, done, info) where:
51                - next_state: The new state observation after taking the action.
52                - reward: The reward received from the environment.
53                - done: A boolean indicating if the episode has ended.
54                - info: A dictionary with any additional information from the environment.
55        """
56
57        ...

Execute the given action and return the resulting experience tuple.

This method should interact with the environment to perform the action, observe the next state, receive the reward, and determine if the episode has terminated.

Args: action: The action to execute in the environment.

Returns: A tuple of (next_state, reward, done, info) where: - next_state: The new state observation after taking the action. - reward: The reward received from the environment. - done: A boolean indicating if the episode has ended. - info: A dictionary with any additional information from the environment.

def update( self, state: tuple, action: Any, reward: float, next_state: tuple) -> None: 
59    def update(self, state: tuple, action: Any, reward: float, next_state: tuple) -> None:
60        """Update the agent's internal model and policy based on experience.
61
62        This method should use the experience tuple (state, action, reward, next_state)
63        to update the agent's learning mechanism, such as updating the HTM model
64        or training a policy network.
65
66        Args:
67            state: The state observed before taking the action.
68            action: The action taken by the agent.
69            reward: The reward received from the environment.
70            next_state: The state observed after taking the action.
71        """
72
73        ...

Update the agent's internal model and policy based on experience.

This method should use the experience tuple (state, action, reward, next_state) to update the agent's learning mechanism, such as updating the HTM model or training a policy network.

Args: state: The state observed before taking the action. action: The action taken by the agent. reward: The reward received from the environment. next_state: The state observed after taking the action.

class Brain: 
 20class Brain:
 21    """Manages HTM input fields and column fields with a unified API.
 22
 23    Allows binding named inputs to InputFields and processing all inputs
 24    with a single `step()` call instead of manually calling encode/compute.
 25
 26    Args:
 27        fields: Optional dictionary of named Field instances to initialize with.
 28            Can include InputField, OutputField, and ColumnField types.
 29
 30    Example:
 31        manager = Trainer()
 32        manager.add_input_field("consumption", consumption_field)
 33        manager.add_input_field("date", date_field)
 34        manager.set_column_field(column_field)
 35
 36        # Training loop - single call replaces manual encode/compute
 37        for row in data:
 38            result = manager.step({
 39                "consumption": row["value"],
 40                "date": row["timestamp"],
 41            })
 42    """
 43
 44    def __init__(self, fields: dict[str, Field] | None = None) -> None:
 45
 46        if fields is None:
 47            fields = {}
 48
 49        # Separate fields into input, output, and column fields for easy access
 50        # ensure that values are instances of the correct type
 51        self._input_fields: dict[str, InputField] = {
 52            k: v for k, v in fields.items() if isinstance(v, InputField)
 53        }
 54        self._output_fields: dict[str, OutputField] = {
 55            k: v for k, v in fields.items() if isinstance(v, OutputField)
 56        }
 57        self._column_fields: dict[str, ColumnField] = {
 58            k: v for k, v in fields.items() if isinstance(v, ColumnField)
 59        }
 60        self.fields = fields
 61        self.logger = get_logger(self)
 62
 63        self.logger.info("Brain initialized with fields: %s", list(fields.keys()))
 64
 65    def __getitem__(self, name: str) -> Field:
 66        return self.fields[name]
 67
 68    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Field:
 69        if name in self.fields:
 70            return self.fields[name]
 71        raise AttributeError(f"'{type(self).__name__}' object has no attribute '{name}'")
 72
 73    def step(
 74        self,
 75        inputs: dict[str, Any],
 76        learn: bool = True,
 77    ):
 78        """Process one timestep: encode all inputs and compute column field.
 79
 80        Args:
 81            inputs: Dict mapping field names to input values.
 82            learn: Whether to enable learning during this step.
 83
 84        Returns:
 85            Dict mapping output field names to their decoded predictions.
 86        """
 87        if learn:
 88            self.logger.info("Processing step with inputs: %s", inputs)
 89
 90        self.encode_only(inputs)
 91        self.compute_only(learn=learn)
 92        return {name: field.decode() for name, field in self._output_fields.items()}
 93
 94    def prediction(self) -> tuple[Any, ...]:
 95        """Get the current prediction for a specific input field.
 96
 97        Returns:
 98            Result from decoder (typically value, confidence tuple).
 99        """
100        predictions = {}
101        for input_name in self._input_fields:
102            input_field = self._input_fields[input_name]
103            if hasattr(input_field.encoder, "decode"):
104                try:
105                    predictions[input_name], predictions[input_name + ".conf"] = input_field.decode(  # type: ignore
106                        "predictive"
107                    )
108                except ValueError:
109                    predictions[input_name] = None
110                    predictions[input_name + ".conf"] = 0.0
111
112        return predictions  # type: ignore
113
114    def rl_policy_update(self) -> None:
115        """RL policy prediction method that returns the next action based on reward fields."""
116        # TODO: Brain needs fully functioning RL reward fields. (GO, NO-GO)
117        pass
118
119    def encode_only(self, inputs: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
120        """Encode inputs without computing (useful for getting predictions first).
121
122        Args:
123            inputs: Dict mapping field names to input values.
124
125        Raises:
126            KeyError: If input field name is not registered.
127        """
128        for name, value in inputs.items():
129            if name not in self._input_fields:
130                raise KeyError(f"Unknown input field: '{name}'")
131            self._input_fields[name].encode(value)
132
133    def compute_only(self, learn: bool = True) -> None:
134        """Compute column field without encoding (inputs already encoded).
135
136        Args:
137            learn: Whether to enable learning during this step.
138        """
139        # Compute temporal memory
140        for column_field in self._column_fields.values():
141            column_field.compute(learn=learn)
142
143    def print_stats(self) -> None:
144        """Print statistics from the column field."""
145        for column_field in self._column_fields.values():
146            self.logger.info("Statistics for ColumnField '%s':", column_field.name)
147            column_field.print_stats()
148
149    def reset(self) -> None:
150        """Clear all states in the column field."""
151        for field in self.fields.values():
152            field.reset()  # type: ignore
153
154    @property
155    def input_fields(self) -> list[InputField]:
156        """Return list of input fields."""
157        return list(self._input_fields.values())
158
159    @property
160    def column_fields(self) -> list[ColumnField]:
161        """Return list of column fields."""
162        return list(self._column_fields.values())
163
164    @property
165    def output_fields(self) -> list[OutputField]:
166        """Return list of output fields."""
167        return list(self._output_fields.values())

Manages HTM input fields and column fields with a unified API.

Allows binding named inputs to InputFields and processing all inputs with a single step() call instead of manually calling encode/compute.

Args: fields: Optional dictionary of named Field instances to initialize with. Can include InputField, OutputField, and ColumnField types.

Example: manager = Trainer() manager.add_input_field("consumption", consumption_field) manager.add_input_field("date", date_field) manager.set_column_field(column_field)

# Training loop - single call replaces manual encode/compute
for row in data:
    result = manager.step({
        "consumption": row["value"],
        "date": row["timestamp"],
    })
Brain(fields: dict[str, Field] | None = None) 
44    def __init__(self, fields: dict[str, Field] | None = None) -> None:
45
46        if fields is None:
47            fields = {}
48
49        # Separate fields into input, output, and column fields for easy access
50        # ensure that values are instances of the correct type
51        self._input_fields: dict[str, InputField] = {
52            k: v for k, v in fields.items() if isinstance(v, InputField)
53        }
54        self._output_fields: dict[str, OutputField] = {
55            k: v for k, v in fields.items() if isinstance(v, OutputField)
56        }
57        self._column_fields: dict[str, ColumnField] = {
58            k: v for k, v in fields.items() if isinstance(v, ColumnField)
59        }
60        self.fields = fields
61        self.logger = get_logger(self)
62
63        self.logger.info("Brain initialized with fields: %s", list(fields.keys()))
fields
logger
def step(self, inputs: dict[str, typing.Any], learn: bool = True): 
73    def step(
74        self,
75        inputs: dict[str, Any],
76        learn: bool = True,
77    ):
78        """Process one timestep: encode all inputs and compute column field.
79
80        Args:
81            inputs: Dict mapping field names to input values.
82            learn: Whether to enable learning during this step.
83
84        Returns:
85            Dict mapping output field names to their decoded predictions.
86        """
87        if learn:
88            self.logger.info("Processing step with inputs: %s", inputs)
89
90        self.encode_only(inputs)
91        self.compute_only(learn=learn)
92        return {name: field.decode() for name, field in self._output_fields.items()}

Process one timestep: encode all inputs and compute column field.

Args: inputs: Dict mapping field names to input values. learn: Whether to enable learning during this step.

Returns: Dict mapping output field names to their decoded predictions.

def prediction(self) -> tuple[typing.Any, ...]: 
 94    def prediction(self) -> tuple[Any, ...]:
 95        """Get the current prediction for a specific input field.
 96
 97        Returns:
 98            Result from decoder (typically value, confidence tuple).
 99        """
100        predictions = {}
101        for input_name in self._input_fields:
102            input_field = self._input_fields[input_name]
103            if hasattr(input_field.encoder, "decode"):
104                try:
105                    predictions[input_name], predictions[input_name + ".conf"] = input_field.decode(  # type: ignore
106                        "predictive"
107                    )
108                except ValueError:
109                    predictions[input_name] = None
110                    predictions[input_name + ".conf"] = 0.0
111
112        return predictions  # type: ignore

Get the current prediction for a specific input field.

Returns: Result from decoder (typically value, confidence tuple).

def rl_policy_update(self) -> None: 
114    def rl_policy_update(self) -> None:
115        """RL policy prediction method that returns the next action based on reward fields."""
116        # TODO: Brain needs fully functioning RL reward fields. (GO, NO-GO)
117        pass

RL policy prediction method that returns the next action based on reward fields.

def encode_only(self, inputs: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> None: 
119    def encode_only(self, inputs: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
120        """Encode inputs without computing (useful for getting predictions first).
121
122        Args:
123            inputs: Dict mapping field names to input values.
124
125        Raises:
126            KeyError: If input field name is not registered.
127        """
128        for name, value in inputs.items():
129            if name not in self._input_fields:
130                raise KeyError(f"Unknown input field: '{name}'")
131            self._input_fields[name].encode(value)

Encode inputs without computing (useful for getting predictions first).

Args: inputs: Dict mapping field names to input values.

Raises: KeyError: If input field name is not registered.

def compute_only(self, learn: bool = True) -> None: 
133    def compute_only(self, learn: bool = True) -> None:
134        """Compute column field without encoding (inputs already encoded).
135
136        Args:
137            learn: Whether to enable learning during this step.
138        """
139        # Compute temporal memory
140        for column_field in self._column_fields.values():
141            column_field.compute(learn=learn)

Compute column field without encoding (inputs already encoded).

Args: learn: Whether to enable learning during this step.

def print_stats(self) -> None: 
143    def print_stats(self) -> None:
144        """Print statistics from the column field."""
145        for column_field in self._column_fields.values():
146            self.logger.info("Statistics for ColumnField '%s':", column_field.name)
147            column_field.print_stats()

Print statistics from the column field.

def reset(self) -> None: 
149    def reset(self) -> None:
150        """Clear all states in the column field."""
151        for field in self.fields.values():
152            field.reset()  # type: ignore

Clear all states in the column field.

input_fields: list[InputField] 
154    @property
155    def input_fields(self) -> list[InputField]:
156        """Return list of input fields."""
157        return list(self._input_fields.values())

Return list of input fields.

column_fields: list[ColumnField] 
159    @property
160    def column_fields(self) -> list[ColumnField]:
161        """Return list of column fields."""
162        return list(self._column_fields.values())

Return list of column fields.

output_fields: list[OutputField] 
164    @property
165    def output_fields(self) -> list[OutputField]:
166        """Return list of output fields."""
167        return list(self._output_fields.values())

Return list of output fields.

class ColumnField(psu_capstone.agent_layer.Field): 
479class ColumnField(Field):
480    """A collection of columns."""
481
482    def __init__(
483        self,
484        input_fields: list[Field],
485        num_columns: int = 0,
486        cells_per_column: int = 1,
487        non_spatial: bool = False,
488        non_temporal: bool = False,
489        duty_cycle_period: int = DUTY_CYCLE_PERIOD,
490    ) -> None:
491        self.num_columns = num_columns
492        self.cells_per_column = cells_per_column
493        self.input_fields: list[Field] = list(input_fields)
494        self.non_spatial = non_spatial
495        self.non_temporal = non_temporal
496        self.duty_cycle_period = max(1, duty_cycle_period)
497        self._duty_cycle_window = 0
498        self._prev_winner_cells: set[Cell] = set()
499        self.initialize()
500
501    def initialize(self) -> None:
502        """Build columns/cells and connect their distal fields for this layer."""
503        self.input_field = Field(chain.from_iterable(self.input_fields))
504        if self.non_temporal:
505            self.cells_per_column = 1
506        if self.non_spatial:
507            num_columns = len(self.input_field.cells)
508            self.columns: list[Column] = [
509                Column(
510                    cells_per_column=self.cells_per_column,
511                )
512                for _ in range(num_columns)
513            ]
514        else:
515            self.columns = [
516                Column(
517                    self.input_field,
518                    cells_per_column=self.cells_per_column,
519                )
520                for _ in range(self.num_columns)
521            ]
522        super().__init__(chain.from_iterable(column.cells for column in self.columns))
523        for column in self.columns:
524            for cell in column.cells:
525                cell.initialize(distal_field=self, apical_field=None)  # type: ignore
526
527        self.clear_states()
528
529    def set_input_fields(self):
530        """Set the input fields for this ColumnField."""
531        self.input_fields = self.input_fields
532        self.initialize()
533
534    def add_input_fields(self, fields: list[Field]) -> None:
535        """Add an input field to this ColumnField."""
536        self.input_fields.extend(fields)
537        additional_cells = Field(chain.from_iterable(field.cells for field in fields))
538        self.input_field.cells.extend(additional_cells)
539        if self.non_spatial:
540            self.columns.extend(
541                Column(cells_per_column=self.cells_per_column)
542                for column in chain.from_iterable(field.cells for field in fields)
543            )
544            for column in self.columns:
545                for cell in column.cells:
546                    cell.initialize(distal_field=self, apical_field=None)  # type: ignore
547        else:
548            for column in self.columns:
549                column.input_field = self.input_field
550                column.receptive_field.union(additional_cells.sample(RECEPTIVE_FIELD_PCT))
551                column.potential_synapses = [
552                    ProximalSynapse(source_cell=cell)
553                    for cell in column.receptive_field
554                    if cell not in [syn.source_cell for syn in column.potential_synapses]
555                ]
556                column._update_connected_synapses()
557
558    def __iter__(self):
559        return iter(self.columns)
560
561    @property
562    def bursting_columns(self) -> list[Column]:
563        """Return list of currently bursting columns."""
564        return [column for column in self.columns if column.bursting]
565
566    @property
567    def active_columns(self) -> list[Column]:
568        """Return list of currently active columns."""
569        return [column for column in self.columns if column.active]
570
571    @property
572    def prev_winner_cells(self) -> set[Cell]:
573        """Return set of previously winning cells in the field."""
574        return self._prev_winner_cells
575
576    def advance_states(self) -> None:
577        """Advance state for this field, columns, and cached previous winner set."""
578        for cls in ColumnField.__mro__:
579            if hasattr(cls, "advance_state") and cls not in (ColumnField, object):
580                cls.advance_state(self)
581        for column in self.columns:
582            column.advance_state()
583        self._prev_winner_cells = set(cell for cell in self.cells if cell.prev_winner)
584
585    def clear_states(self) -> None:
586        """Clear current and previous state flags for this field hierarchy."""
587        for cls in ColumnField.__mro__:
588            if hasattr(cls, "clear_state") and cls not in (ColumnField, object):
589                cls.clear_state(self)
590        for column in self.columns:
591            column.clear_state()
592        self._prev_winner_cells = set()
593
594    def compute(self, learn=True) -> None:
595        """Run one HTM timestep including spatial, temporal, and learning phases."""
596        self.advance_states()
597
598        if self.non_spatial:
599            for column, input_cell in zip(self.columns, self.input_field.cells):
600                if input_cell.active:
601                    column.set_active()  # type: ignore
602        else:
603            for column in self.columns:
604                column.compute_overlap()
605
606            self.activate_columns()
607
608            if learn:
609                self.learn_columns()
610
611        if self.non_temporal:
612            for column in self.active_columns:
613                for cell in column.cells:
614                    cell.set_active()  # type: ignore
615        else:
616            self.activate_cells()
617
618            self.depolarize_cells()
619
620            if learn:
621                self.learn()
622
623        self.set_prediction()
624
625        self._update_duty_cycles()
626
627    def activate_columns(self) -> None:
628        """Activate columns according to configured local sparsity."""
629        self.activate_top_k_columns(int(len(self.columns) * DESIRED_LOCAL_SPARSITY))
630
631    def learn_columns(self) -> None:
632        """Apply proximal learning updates to currently active columns."""
633        for column in self.active_columns:
634            column.learn()
635
636    def activate_top_k_columns(self, k: int) -> None:
637        """Activate the top-k columns based on overlap."""
638        sorted_columns = sorted(self.columns, key=lambda col: col.overlap, reverse=True)
639        for col in sorted_columns[:k]:
640            self.active_columns.append(col)
641            col.set_active()  # type: ignore
642
643    def activate_cells(self) -> None:
644        """Activate, burst, and select winner cells based on prior predictions."""
645        for column in self.active_columns:
646            if any(cell.prev_predictive for cell in column.cells):  # Same as 1) L3
647                column.set_predictive()  # type: ignore
648                for segment in column.segments:
649                    if segment.prev_active:  # Same as 1) L11
650                        segment.parent_cell.set_active()  # type: ignore
651                        segment.parent_cell.set_winner()  # type: ignore
652                        segment.set_learning()  # type: ignore
653
654            if not any(cell.prev_predictive for cell in column.cells):  # Same as 1) L5
655                column.set_bursting()  # type: ignore
656                for cell in column.cells:
657                    cell.set_active()  # type: ignore
658                if any(segment.prev_matching for segment in column.segments):  # Same as 1) L29
659                    learning_segment = column.best_potential_prev_active_segment()  # Same as 1) L30
660                    winner_cell = learning_segment.parent_cell  # type: ignore
661                else:
662                    winner_cell = column.least_used_cell
663                    learning_segment = Segment(parent_cell=winner_cell)
664                    winner_cell.segments.append(learning_segment)  # Same as 1) L35
665                    # learning_apical_segment = ApicalSegment(parent_cell=winner_cell)
666                    # winner_cell.segments.append(learning_apical_segment)
667
668                winner_cell.set_winner()  # type: ignore
669                learning_segment.set_learning()  # type: ignore
670
671    def depolarize_cells(self) -> None:
672        """Evaluate all segments to set matching/active/predictive states."""
673        for column in self.columns:
674            for segment in column.segments:
675                segment.activate_segment()
676
677    def learn(self) -> None:
678        """Apply distal/apical learning rules for active, bursting, and matching segments."""
679        for column in self.active_columns:
680            if not column.bursting:
681                for segment in column.segments:
682                    if segment.learning:
683                        segment.grow()  # Same as 1) L22-24
684                        segment.adapt()  # Same as 1) L16-20
685
686        for column in self.bursting_columns:
687            for segment in column.segments:
688                if segment.learning:  # Same as 1) L40-48
689                    segment.grow()
690                    segment.adapt(strength=1.0)  # Same as 1) L42-44
691
692        for column in self.columns:
693            if not column.active:
694                for segment in column.segments:
695                    if segment.matching:
696                        segment.weaken(PREDICTED_DECREMENT_PCT)  # Same as 1) L25-27
697
698    def set_prediction(self) -> list[Field]:  # type: ignore
699        """Return column-level predictive state and update source fields."""
700        if self.non_spatial:
701            for column, input_cell in zip(self.columns, self.input_field):
702                if any(cell.predictive for cell in column.cells):
703                    input_cell.set_predictive()  # type: ignore
704
705            return self.input_fields
706
707    def _update_duty_cycles(self) -> None:
708        self._duty_cycle_window = min(self.duty_cycle_period, self._duty_cycle_window + 1)
709        alpha = 1.0 / self._duty_cycle_window
710        for column in self.columns:
711            column.active_duty_cycle += alpha * (
712                (1.0 if column.active else 0.0) - column.active_duty_cycle
713            )
714        for cell in self.cells:
715            cell.active_duty_cycle += alpha * (
716                (1.0 if cell.active else 0.0) - cell.active_duty_cycle
717            )
718
719    def print_stats(self) -> None:
720        """Print statistics about the current stats (with stddev) of the segments  and synapses in the ColumnField."""
721
722        def describe(values: list[float]) -> tuple[int, float, float, float, float]:
723            if not values:
724                return 0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0
725            count = len(values)
726            mean_val = fmean(values)
727            std_val = pstdev(values) if count > 1 else 0.0
728            return count, mean_val, std_val, min(values), max(values)
729
730        def format_metric(
731            label: str,
732            stats: tuple[int, float, float, float, float],
733            value_precision: str = ".2f",
734            extrema_precision: str = ".0f",
735        ) -> str:
736            _, mean_val, std_val, min_val, max_val = stats
737            mean_str = format(mean_val, value_precision)
738            std_str = format(std_val, value_precision)
739            min_str = format(min_val, extrema_precision)
740            max_str = format(max_val, extrema_precision)
741            return f"| {label:<22}| {mean_str:>8} ± {std_str:<8}| {min_str:>8} | {max_str:>8} |"
742
743        segments_per_cell = [len(cell.segments) for cell in self.cells]
744        all_segments = [segment for cell in self.cells for segment in cell.segments]
745        synapses_per_segment = [len(segment.synapses) for segment in all_segments]
746        all_synapses = [syn for segment in all_segments for syn in segment.synapses]
747        permanences = [syn.permanence for syn in all_synapses]
748        column_duty_cycles = [column.active_duty_cycle for column in self.columns]
749        cell_duty_cycles = [cell.active_duty_cycle for cell in self.cells]
750
751        seg_count, seg_mean, seg_std, seg_min, seg_max = describe(segments_per_cell)  # type: ignore
752        syn_count, syn_mean, syn_std, syn_min, syn_max = describe(synapses_per_segment)  # type: ignore
753        perm_count, perm_mean, perm_std, perm_min, perm_max = describe(permanences)
754        col_duty_stats = describe(column_duty_cycles)
755        cell_duty_stats = describe(cell_duty_cycles)
756
757        connected_synapses = sum(1 for syn in all_synapses if syn.permanence >= CONNECTED_PERM)
758        connected_ratio = (connected_synapses / perm_count) if perm_count else 0.0
759        active_columns = sum(1 for duty in column_duty_cycles if duty > 0.0)
760        active_cells = sum(1 for duty in cell_duty_cycles if duty > 0.0)
761        column_share = (active_columns / len(self.columns)) if self.columns else 0.0
762        cell_share = (active_cells / len(self.cells)) if self.cells else 0.0
763
764        table_lines = [
765            "+------------------------+--------------------+----------+----------+",
766            "| Metric                 |   Mean ± Std      |      Min |      Max |",
767            "+------------------------+--------------------+----------+----------+",
768            format_metric("Segments per cell", (seg_count, seg_mean, seg_std, seg_min, seg_max)),
769            format_metric("Synapses per segment", (syn_count, syn_mean, syn_std, syn_min, syn_max)),
770            format_metric(
771                "Permanence",
772                (perm_count, perm_mean, perm_std, perm_min, perm_max),
773                value_precision=".3f",
774                extrema_precision=".3f",
775            ),
776            format_metric(
777                "Column duty cycle",
778                col_duty_stats,
779                value_precision=".3f",
780                extrema_precision=".3f",
781            ),
782            format_metric(
783                "Cell duty cycle",
784                cell_duty_stats,
785                value_precision=".3f",
786                extrema_precision=".3f",
787            ),
788            "+------------------------+--------------------+----------+----------+",
789        ]
790
791        print("ColumnField statistics:")
792        print(
793            f"  Columns: {len(self.columns)} | Cells: {len(self.cells)} | Segments: {len(all_segments)} | Synapses: {len(all_synapses)}"
794        )
795        for line in table_lines:
796            print(f"  {line}")
797        print(
798            f"  Connected synapses (>= {CONNECTED_PERM}): {connected_synapses}"
799            f" ({connected_ratio:.1%} of all synapses)"
800        )
801        print(f"  Columns with duty > 0: {active_columns}/{len(self.columns)} ({column_share:.1%})")
802        print(f"  Cells with duty > 0: {active_cells}/{len(self.cells)} ({cell_share:.1%})")

A collection of columns.

ColumnField( input_fields: list[Field], num_columns: int = 0, cells_per_column: int = 1, non_spatial: bool = False, non_temporal: bool = False, duty_cycle_period: int = 1000) 
482    def __init__(
483        self,
484        input_fields: list[Field],
485        num_columns: int = 0,
486        cells_per_column: int = 1,
487        non_spatial: bool = False,
488        non_temporal: bool = False,
489        duty_cycle_period: int = DUTY_CYCLE_PERIOD,
490    ) -> None:
491        self.num_columns = num_columns
492        self.cells_per_column = cells_per_column
493        self.input_fields: list[Field] = list(input_fields)
494        self.non_spatial = non_spatial
495        self.non_temporal = non_temporal
496        self.duty_cycle_period = max(1, duty_cycle_period)
497        self._duty_cycle_window = 0
498        self._prev_winner_cells: set[Cell] = set()
499        self.initialize()
num_columns
cells_per_column
input_fields: list[Field]
non_spatial
non_temporal
duty_cycle_period
def initialize(self) -> None: 
501    def initialize(self) -> None:
502        """Build columns/cells and connect their distal fields for this layer."""
503        self.input_field = Field(chain.from_iterable(self.input_fields))
504        if self.non_temporal:
505            self.cells_per_column = 1
506        if self.non_spatial:
507            num_columns = len(self.input_field.cells)
508            self.columns: list[Column] = [
509                Column(
510                    cells_per_column=self.cells_per_column,
511                )
512                for _ in range(num_columns)
513            ]
514        else:
515            self.columns = [
516                Column(
517                    self.input_field,
