518                    cells_per_column=self.cells_per_column,
519                )
520                for _ in range(self.num_columns)
521            ]
522        super().__init__(chain.from_iterable(column.cells for column in self.columns))
523        for column in self.columns:
524            for cell in column.cells:
525                cell.initialize(distal_field=self, apical_field=None)  # type: ignore
526
527        self.clear_states()

Build columns/cells and connect their distal fields for this layer.

def set_input_fields(self): 
529    def set_input_fields(self):
530        """Set the input fields for this ColumnField."""
531        self.input_fields = self.input_fields
532        self.initialize()

Set the input fields for this ColumnField.

def add_input_fields(self, fields: list[Field]) -> None: 
534    def add_input_fields(self, fields: list[Field]) -> None:
535        """Add an input field to this ColumnField."""
536        self.input_fields.extend(fields)
537        additional_cells = Field(chain.from_iterable(field.cells for field in fields))
538        self.input_field.cells.extend(additional_cells)
539        if self.non_spatial:
540            self.columns.extend(
541                Column(cells_per_column=self.cells_per_column)
542                for column in chain.from_iterable(field.cells for field in fields)
543            )
544            for column in self.columns:
545                for cell in column.cells:
546                    cell.initialize(distal_field=self, apical_field=None)  # type: ignore
547        else:
548            for column in self.columns:
549                column.input_field = self.input_field
550                column.receptive_field.union(additional_cells.sample(RECEPTIVE_FIELD_PCT))
551                column.potential_synapses = [
552                    ProximalSynapse(source_cell=cell)
553                    for cell in column.receptive_field
554                    if cell not in [syn.source_cell for syn in column.potential_synapses]
555                ]
556                column._update_connected_synapses()

Add an input field to this ColumnField.

bursting_columns: list[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Column] 
561    @property
562    def bursting_columns(self) -> list[Column]:
563        """Return list of currently bursting columns."""
564        return [column for column in self.columns if column.bursting]

Return list of currently bursting columns.

active_columns: list[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Column] 
566    @property
567    def active_columns(self) -> list[Column]:
568        """Return list of currently active columns."""
569        return [column for column in self.columns if column.active]

Return list of currently active columns.

prev_winner_cells: set[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Cell] 
571    @property
572    def prev_winner_cells(self) -> set[Cell]:
573        """Return set of previously winning cells in the field."""
574        return self._prev_winner_cells

Return set of previously winning cells in the field.

def advance_states(self) -> None: 
576    def advance_states(self) -> None:
577        """Advance state for this field, columns, and cached previous winner set."""
578        for cls in ColumnField.__mro__:
579            if hasattr(cls, "advance_state") and cls not in (ColumnField, object):
580                cls.advance_state(self)
581        for column in self.columns:
582            column.advance_state()
583        self._prev_winner_cells = set(cell for cell in self.cells if cell.prev_winner)

Advance state for this field, columns, and cached previous winner set.

def clear_states(self) -> None: 
585    def clear_states(self) -> None:
586        """Clear current and previous state flags for this field hierarchy."""
587        for cls in ColumnField.__mro__:
588            if hasattr(cls, "clear_state") and cls not in (ColumnField, object):
589                cls.clear_state(self)
590        for column in self.columns:
591            column.clear_state()
592        self._prev_winner_cells = set()

Clear current and previous state flags for this field hierarchy.

def compute(self, learn=True) -> None: 
594    def compute(self, learn=True) -> None:
595        """Run one HTM timestep including spatial, temporal, and learning phases."""
596        self.advance_states()
597
598        if self.non_spatial:
599            for column, input_cell in zip(self.columns, self.input_field.cells):
600                if input_cell.active:
601                    column.set_active()  # type: ignore
602        else:
603            for column in self.columns:
604                column.compute_overlap()
605
606            self.activate_columns()
607
608            if learn:
609                self.learn_columns()
610
611        if self.non_temporal:
612            for column in self.active_columns:
613                for cell in column.cells:
614                    cell.set_active()  # type: ignore
615        else:
616            self.activate_cells()
617
618            self.depolarize_cells()
619
620            if learn:
621                self.learn()
622
623        self.set_prediction()
624
625        self._update_duty_cycles()

Run one HTM timestep including spatial, temporal, and learning phases.

def activate_columns(self) -> None: 
627    def activate_columns(self) -> None:
628        """Activate columns according to configured local sparsity."""
629        self.activate_top_k_columns(int(len(self.columns) * DESIRED_LOCAL_SPARSITY))

Activate columns according to configured local sparsity.

def learn_columns(self) -> None: 
631    def learn_columns(self) -> None:
632        """Apply proximal learning updates to currently active columns."""
633        for column in self.active_columns:
634            column.learn()

Apply proximal learning updates to currently active columns.

def activate_top_k_columns(self, k: int) -> None: 
636    def activate_top_k_columns(self, k: int) -> None:
637        """Activate the top-k columns based on overlap."""
638        sorted_columns = sorted(self.columns, key=lambda col: col.overlap, reverse=True)
639        for col in sorted_columns[:k]:
640            self.active_columns.append(col)
641            col.set_active()  # type: ignore

Activate the top-k columns based on overlap.

def activate_cells(self) -> None: 
643    def activate_cells(self) -> None:
644        """Activate, burst, and select winner cells based on prior predictions."""
645        for column in self.active_columns:
646            if any(cell.prev_predictive for cell in column.cells):  # Same as 1) L3
647                column.set_predictive()  # type: ignore
648                for segment in column.segments:
649                    if segment.prev_active:  # Same as 1) L11
650                        segment.parent_cell.set_active()  # type: ignore
651                        segment.parent_cell.set_winner()  # type: ignore
652                        segment.set_learning()  # type: ignore
653
654            if not any(cell.prev_predictive for cell in column.cells):  # Same as 1) L5
655                column.set_bursting()  # type: ignore
656                for cell in column.cells:
657                    cell.set_active()  # type: ignore
658                if any(segment.prev_matching for segment in column.segments):  # Same as 1) L29
659                    learning_segment = column.best_potential_prev_active_segment()  # Same as 1) L30
660                    winner_cell = learning_segment.parent_cell  # type: ignore
661                else:
662                    winner_cell = column.least_used_cell
663                    learning_segment = Segment(parent_cell=winner_cell)
664                    winner_cell.segments.append(learning_segment)  # Same as 1) L35
665                    # learning_apical_segment = ApicalSegment(parent_cell=winner_cell)
666                    # winner_cell.segments.append(learning_apical_segment)
667
668                winner_cell.set_winner()  # type: ignore
669                learning_segment.set_learning()  # type: ignore

Activate, burst, and select winner cells based on prior predictions.

def depolarize_cells(self) -> None: 
671    def depolarize_cells(self) -> None:
672        """Evaluate all segments to set matching/active/predictive states."""
673        for column in self.columns:
674            for segment in column.segments:
675                segment.activate_segment()

Evaluate all segments to set matching/active/predictive states.

def learn(self) -> None: 
677    def learn(self) -> None:
678        """Apply distal/apical learning rules for active, bursting, and matching segments."""
679        for column in self.active_columns:
680            if not column.bursting:
681                for segment in column.segments:
682                    if segment.learning:
683                        segment.grow()  # Same as 1) L22-24
684                        segment.adapt()  # Same as 1) L16-20
685
686        for column in self.bursting_columns:
687            for segment in column.segments:
688                if segment.learning:  # Same as 1) L40-48
689                    segment.grow()
690                    segment.adapt(strength=1.0)  # Same as 1) L42-44
691
692        for column in self.columns:
693            if not column.active:
694                for segment in column.segments:
695                    if segment.matching:
696                        segment.weaken(PREDICTED_DECREMENT_PCT)  # Same as 1) L25-27

Apply distal/apical learning rules for active, bursting, and matching segments.

def set_prediction(self) -> list[Field]: 
698    def set_prediction(self) -> list[Field]:  # type: ignore
699        """Return column-level predictive state and update source fields."""
700        if self.non_spatial:
701            for column, input_cell in zip(self.columns, self.input_field):
702                if any(cell.predictive for cell in column.cells):
703                    input_cell.set_predictive()  # type: ignore
704
705            return self.input_fields

Return column-level predictive state and update source fields.

def print_stats(self) -> None: 
719    def print_stats(self) -> None:
720        """Print statistics about the current stats (with stddev) of the segments  and synapses in the ColumnField."""
721
722        def describe(values: list[float]) -> tuple[int, float, float, float, float]:
723            if not values:
724                return 0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0
725            count = len(values)
726            mean_val = fmean(values)
727            std_val = pstdev(values) if count > 1 else 0.0
728            return count, mean_val, std_val, min(values), max(values)
729
730        def format_metric(
731            label: str,
732            stats: tuple[int, float, float, float, float],
733            value_precision: str = ".2f",
734            extrema_precision: str = ".0f",
735        ) -> str:
736            _, mean_val, std_val, min_val, max_val = stats
737            mean_str = format(mean_val, value_precision)
738            std_str = format(std_val, value_precision)
739            min_str = format(min_val, extrema_precision)
740            max_str = format(max_val, extrema_precision)
741            return f"| {label:<22}| {mean_str:>8} ± {std_str:<8}| {min_str:>8} | {max_str:>8} |"
742
743        segments_per_cell = [len(cell.segments) for cell in self.cells]
744        all_segments = [segment for cell in self.cells for segment in cell.segments]
745        synapses_per_segment = [len(segment.synapses) for segment in all_segments]
746        all_synapses = [syn for segment in all_segments for syn in segment.synapses]
747        permanences = [syn.permanence for syn in all_synapses]
748        column_duty_cycles = [column.active_duty_cycle for column in self.columns]
749        cell_duty_cycles = [cell.active_duty_cycle for cell in self.cells]
750
751        seg_count, seg_mean, seg_std, seg_min, seg_max = describe(segments_per_cell)  # type: ignore
752        syn_count, syn_mean, syn_std, syn_min, syn_max = describe(synapses_per_segment)  # type: ignore
753        perm_count, perm_mean, perm_std, perm_min, perm_max = describe(permanences)
754        col_duty_stats = describe(column_duty_cycles)
755        cell_duty_stats = describe(cell_duty_cycles)
756
757        connected_synapses = sum(1 for syn in all_synapses if syn.permanence >= CONNECTED_PERM)
758        connected_ratio = (connected_synapses / perm_count) if perm_count else 0.0
759        active_columns = sum(1 for duty in column_duty_cycles if duty > 0.0)
760        active_cells = sum(1 for duty in cell_duty_cycles if duty > 0.0)
761        column_share = (active_columns / len(self.columns)) if self.columns else 0.0
762        cell_share = (active_cells / len(self.cells)) if self.cells else 0.0
763
764        table_lines = [
765            "+------------------------+--------------------+----------+----------+",
766            "| Metric                 |   Mean ± Std      |      Min |      Max |",
767            "+------------------------+--------------------+----------+----------+",
768            format_metric("Segments per cell", (seg_count, seg_mean, seg_std, seg_min, seg_max)),
769            format_metric("Synapses per segment", (syn_count, syn_mean, syn_std, syn_min, syn_max)),
770            format_metric(
771                "Permanence",
772                (perm_count, perm_mean, perm_std, perm_min, perm_max),
773                value_precision=".3f",
774                extrema_precision=".3f",
775            ),
776            format_metric(
777                "Column duty cycle",
778                col_duty_stats,
779                value_precision=".3f",
780                extrema_precision=".3f",
781            ),
782            format_metric(
783                "Cell duty cycle",
784                cell_duty_stats,
785                value_precision=".3f",
786                extrema_precision=".3f",
787            ),
788            "+------------------------+--------------------+----------+----------+",
789        ]
790
791        print("ColumnField statistics:")
792        print(
793            f"  Columns: {len(self.columns)} | Cells: {len(self.cells)} | Segments: {len(all_segments)} | Synapses: {len(all_synapses)}"
794        )
795        for line in table_lines:
796            print(f"  {line}")
797        print(
798            f"  Connected synapses (>= {CONNECTED_PERM}): {connected_synapses}"
799            f" ({connected_ratio:.1%} of all synapses)"
800        )
801        print(f"  Columns with duty > 0: {active_columns}/{len(self.columns)} ({column_share:.1%})")
802        print(f"  Cells with duty > 0: {active_cells}/{len(self.cells)} ({cell_share:.1%})")

Print statistics about the current stats (with stddev) of the segments and synapses in the ColumnField.

class Field: 
 88class Field:
 89    """A collection of cells."""
 90
 91    def __init__(self, cells: Iterable["Cell"]) -> None:
 92        self.cells: list["Cell"] = list(cells)
 93        self._name: str = ""
 94
 95    def __iter__(self):
 96        return iter(self.cells)
 97
 98    def sample(self, pct: float) -> set["Cell"]:
 99        """Sample 'pct' percent cells from the field."""
100        n = int(len(self.cells) * pct)
101        if n > len(self.cells):
102            raise ValueError("Cannot sample more cells than are in the field.")
103        return set(random.sample(self.cells, n))
104
105    @property
106    def name(self) -> str:
107        return self._name
108
109    @name.setter
110    def name(self, value: str) -> None:
111        if not isinstance(value, str):
112            raise ValueError("Field name must be a string.")
113        self._name = value
114
115    @property
116    def active_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
117        """Return set of previously active cells in the field."""
118        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.active}
119
120    @property
121    def prev_active_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
122        """Return set of previously active cells in the field."""
123        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.prev_active}
124
125    @property
126    def predictive_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
127        """Return set of previously active cells in the field."""
128        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.predictive}
129
130    @property
131    def prev_predictive_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
132        """Return set of previously predictive cells in the field."""
133        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.prev_predictive}
134
135    @property
136    def prev_learning_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
137        """Return set of previously learning cells in the field."""
138        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.prev_learning}  # type: ignore
139
140    @property
141    def prev_winner_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
142        """Return set of previously winning cells in the field."""
143        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.prev_winner}

A collection of cells.

Field(cells: Iterable[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Cell]) 
91    def __init__(self, cells: Iterable["Cell"]) -> None:
92        self.cells: list["Cell"] = list(cells)
93        self._name: str = ""
cells: list[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Cell]
def sample(self, pct: float) -> set[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Cell]: 
 98    def sample(self, pct: float) -> set["Cell"]:
 99        """Sample 'pct' percent cells from the field."""
100        n = int(len(self.cells) * pct)
101        if n > len(self.cells):
102            raise ValueError("Cannot sample more cells than are in the field.")
103        return set(random.sample(self.cells, n))

Sample 'pct' percent cells from the field.

name: str 
105    @property
106    def name(self) -> str:
107        return self._name
active_cells: set[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Cell] 
115    @property
116    def active_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
117        """Return set of previously active cells in the field."""
118        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.active}

Return set of previously active cells in the field.

prev_active_cells: set[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Cell] 
120    @property
121    def prev_active_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
122        """Return set of previously active cells in the field."""
123        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.prev_active}

Return set of previously active cells in the field.

predictive_cells: set[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Cell] 
125    @property
126    def predictive_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
127        """Return set of previously active cells in the field."""
128        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.predictive}

Return set of previously active cells in the field.

prev_predictive_cells: set[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Cell] 
130    @property
131    def prev_predictive_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
132        """Return set of previously predictive cells in the field."""
133        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.prev_predictive}

Return set of previously predictive cells in the field.

prev_learning_cells: set[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Cell] 
135    @property
136    def prev_learning_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
137        """Return set of previously learning cells in the field."""
138        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.prev_learning}  # type: ignore

Return set of previously learning cells in the field.

prev_winner_cells: set[psu_capstone.agent_layer.HTM.Cell] 
140    @property
141    def prev_winner_cells(self) -> set["Cell"]:
142        """Return set of previously winning cells in the field."""
143        return {cell for cell in self.cells if cell.prev_winner}

Return set of previously winning cells in the field.

class InputField(psu_capstone.agent_layer.Field): 
805class InputField(Field):
806    """A Field specialized for input bits with an encoder.
807
808    Encapsulates input encoding by wrapping an encoder (default RDSE) and
809    managing a Field of cells corresponding to the encoder's output bits.
810
811    Args:
812        encoder_params: Configuration parameters for the encoder. If None
813            or not a ParameterMarker-compatible object, defaults to RDSEParameters.
814        size: Optional size override for the encoder output. If provided,
815            overrides the size parameter in encoder_params.
816    """
817
818    def __init__(self, encoder_params: Any | None = None, size: int | None = None) -> None:
819        if encoder_params is not None and isinstance(encoder_params, ParameterMarker):
820            params = copy.deepcopy(encoder_params)
821        else:
822            params = RDSEParameters()
823
824        if size is not None and hasattr(params, "size") and size > 0:
825            params.size = size
826
827        self._encoder = params.encoder_class(params)  # type: ignore
828
829        cells = {Cell() for _ in range(self._encoder.size)}
830        Field.__init__(self, cells)
831
832    @property
833    def encoder(self) -> Any:
834        """Return the encoder bound to this input field."""
835
836        if self._encoder is None:
837
838            raise ValueError("Encoder is not set.")
839
840        if hasattr(self._encoder, "size") and len(self.cells) != self._encoder.size:
841            raise ValueError(
842                f"Encoder size {self._encoder.size} does not match number of cells {len(self.cells)}"
843            )
844        return self._encoder
845
846    @encoder.setter
847    def encoder(self, encoder: Any) -> None:
848        """Set the field encoder and keep cell count aligned with encoder size."""
849
850        if encoder is None or not hasattr(encoder, "size"):
851            raise ValueError("Encoder must define a 'size' attribute.")
852
853        if hasattr(encoder, "size") and len(self.cells) != encoder.size:
854            raise ValueError(
855                f"Encoder size {encoder.size} does not match number of cells {len(self.cells)}"
856            )
857
858        self._encoder = encoder
859
860    def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]:
861        """Encode the input value into a binary vector."""
862
863        self.advance_states()
864        encoded_bits = self.encoder.encode(input_value)
865        for idx, cell in enumerate(self.cells):
866            if encoded_bits[idx]:
867                cell.set_active()  # type: ignore
868        return encoded_bits
869
870    def decode(
871        self,
872        state: str = "active",
873        encoded: Field = None,  # type: ignore
874        candidates: Iterable[float] | None = None,
875    ) -> tuple[float | None] | dict[str, tuple[float | None]]:
876        """Convert active cells back to input value using RDSE decoding."""
877        if state not in ("active", "predictive"):
878            raise ValueError(f"Invalid state '{state}'; must be 'active' or 'predictive'")
879        if encoded is None:
880            encoded = self.cells
881        bit_vector = [getattr(cell, state) for cell in encoded]
882        return self.encoder.decode(bit_vector, candidates)  # type: ignore
883
884    def advance_states(self) -> None:
885        """Advance state on all input cells before writing a new encoding."""
886        for cell in self.cells:
887            cell.advance_state()
888
889    def clear_states(self) -> None:
890        """Clear state on all input cells."""
891        for cell in self.cells:
892            cell.clear_state()

A Field specialized for input bits with an encoder.

Encapsulates input encoding by wrapping an encoder (default RDSE) and managing a Field of cells corresponding to the encoder's output bits.

Args: encoder_params: Configuration parameters for the encoder. If None or not a ParameterMarker-compatible object, defaults to RDSEParameters. size: Optional size override for the encoder output. If provided, overrides the size parameter in encoder_params.

InputField(encoder_params: typing.Any | None = None, size: int | None = None) 
818    def __init__(self, encoder_params: Any | None = None, size: int | None = None) -> None:
819        if encoder_params is not None and isinstance(encoder_params, ParameterMarker):
820            params = copy.deepcopy(encoder_params)
821        else:
822            params = RDSEParameters()
823
824        if size is not None and hasattr(params, "size") and size > 0:
825            params.size = size
826
827        self._encoder = params.encoder_class(params)  # type: ignore
828
829        cells = {Cell() for _ in range(self._encoder.size)}
830        Field.__init__(self, cells)
encoder: Any 
832    @property
833    def encoder(self) -> Any:
834        """Return the encoder bound to this input field."""
835
836        if self._encoder is None:
837
838            raise ValueError("Encoder is not set.")
839
840        if hasattr(self._encoder, "size") and len(self.cells) != self._encoder.size:
841            raise ValueError(
842                f"Encoder size {self._encoder.size} does not match number of cells {len(self.cells)}"
843            )
844        return self._encoder

Return the encoder bound to this input field.

def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]: 
860    def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]:
861        """Encode the input value into a binary vector."""
862
863        self.advance_states()
864        encoded_bits = self.encoder.encode(input_value)
865        for idx, cell in enumerate(self.cells):
866            if encoded_bits[idx]:
867                cell.set_active()  # type: ignore
868        return encoded_bits

Encode the input value into a binary vector.

def decode( self, state: str = 'active', encoded: Field = None, candidates: Optional[Iterable[float]] = None) -> tuple[float | None] | dict[str, tuple[float | None]]: 
870    def decode(
871        self,
872        state: str = "active",
873        encoded: Field = None,  # type: ignore
874        candidates: Iterable[float] | None = None,
875    ) -> tuple[float | None] | dict[str, tuple[float | None]]:
876        """Convert active cells back to input value using RDSE decoding."""
877        if state not in ("active", "predictive"):
878            raise ValueError(f"Invalid state '{state}'; must be 'active' or 'predictive'")
879        if encoded is None:
880            encoded = self.cells
881        bit_vector = [getattr(cell, state) for cell in encoded]
882        return self.encoder.decode(bit_vector, candidates)  # type: ignore

Convert active cells back to input value using RDSE decoding.

def advance_states(self) -> None: 
884    def advance_states(self) -> None:
885        """Advance state on all input cells before writing a new encoding."""
886        for cell in self.cells:
887            cell.advance_state()

Advance state on all input cells before writing a new encoding.

def clear_states(self) -> None: 
889    def clear_states(self) -> None:
890        """Clear state on all input cells."""
891        for cell in self.cells:
892            cell.clear_state()

Clear state on all input cells.

class OutputField(psu_capstone.agent_layer.Field): 
895class OutputField(Field):
896    """A Field specialized for output bits."""
897
898    def __init__(self, size: int, motor_action: tuple) -> None:
899        cells = {Cell() for _ in range(size)}
900        Field.__init__(self, cells)
901        self.motor_action = motor_action
902
903    def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]:
904        """Encode the input value into a binary vector."""
905        raise NotImplementedError("OutputField does not support encoding")
906
907    def decode(
908        self,
909        state: str = "active",
910        encoded: Field = None,  # type: ignore
911        _candidates: Iterable[float] | None = None,
912    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
913        """Map output cell activity into a motor action payload.
914
915        Returns a lightweight dictionary so Brain.step can expose a direct
916        action hint to Agent policy code.
917        """
918        if state not in ("active", "predictive"):
919            raise ValueError(f"Invalid state '{state}'; must be 'active' or 'predictive'")
920
921        if encoded is None:
922            encoded = self.cells
923
924        cells = list(encoded)
925        if not cells:
926            return {"action": None, "confidence": 0.0}
927
928        active_indices = [idx for idx, cell in enumerate(cells) if bool(getattr(cell, state))]
929
930        # In active-mode decoding, allow predictive bits to provide a weak
931        # action hint when no active bits are currently set.
932        if state == "active" and not active_indices:
933            predictive_indices = [idx for idx, cell in enumerate(cells) if bool(cell.predictive)]
934            if predictive_indices:
935                active_indices = predictive_indices
936
937        confidence = len(active_indices) / len(cells)
938
939        # Treat motor_action as an ordered action candidate tuple.
940        if not self.motor_action:
941            return {"action": None, "confidence": confidence}
942
943        if not active_indices:
944            return {"action": self.motor_action[0], "confidence": 0.0}
945
946        selected_index = max(active_indices) % len(self.motor_action)
947        return {
948            "action": self.motor_action[selected_index],
949            "confidence": confidence,
950        }

A Field specialized for output bits.

OutputField(size: int, motor_action: tuple) 
898    def __init__(self, size: int, motor_action: tuple) -> None:
899        cells = {Cell() for _ in range(size)}
900        Field.__init__(self, cells)
901        self.motor_action = motor_action
motor_action
def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]: 
903    def encode(self, input_value: Any) -> list[int]:
904        """Encode the input value into a binary vector."""
905        raise NotImplementedError("OutputField does not support encoding")

Encode the input value into a binary vector.

def decode( self, state: str = 'active', encoded: Field = None, _candidates: Optional[Iterable[float]] = None) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
907    def decode(
908        self,
909        state: str = "active",
910        encoded: Field = None,  # type: ignore
911        _candidates: Iterable[float] | None = None,
912    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
913        """Map output cell activity into a motor action payload.
914
915        Returns a lightweight dictionary so Brain.step can expose a direct
916        action hint to Agent policy code.
917        """
918        if state not in ("active", "predictive"):
919            raise ValueError(f"Invalid state '{state}'; must be 'active' or 'predictive'")
920
921        if encoded is None:
922            encoded = self.cells
923
924        cells = list(encoded)
925        if not cells:
926            return {"action": None, "confidence": 0.0}
927
928        active_indices = [idx for idx, cell in enumerate(cells) if bool(getattr(cell, state))]
929
930        # In active-mode decoding, allow predictive bits to provide a weak
931        # action hint when no active bits are currently set.
932        if state == "active" and not active_indices:
933            predictive_indices = [idx for idx, cell in enumerate(cells) if bool(cell.predictive)]
934            if predictive_indices:
935                active_indices = predictive_indices
936
937        confidence = len(active_indices) / len(cells)
938
939        # Treat motor_action as an ordered action candidate tuple.
940        if not self.motor_action:
941            return {"action": None, "confidence": confidence}
942
943        if not active_indices:
944            return {"action": self.motor_action[0], "confidence": 0.0}
945
946        selected_index = max(active_indices) % len(self.motor_action)
947        return {
948            "action": self.motor_action[selected_index],
949            "confidence": confidence,
950        }

Map output cell activity into a motor action payload.

Returns a lightweight dictionary so Brain.step can expose a direct action hint to Agent policy code.

class Trainer: 
 39class Trainer:
 40    """Build a Trainer for training Brains on a dataset.
 41
 42    Args:
 43        brain: Brain instance to be trained with this trainer.
 44
 45    Attributes:
 46        brain_blueprint: Blueprint class used for creating Brain instances.
 47        input_field_blueprint: Blueprint class used for creating input fields.
 48        output_field_blueprint: Blueprint class used for creating output fields.
 49        column_field_blueprint: Blueprint class used for creating column fields.
 50    """
 51
 52    _BRAIN_NOT_INITIALIZED_ERROR = "Main Brain is not initialized. Please build the Brain first."
 53
 54    # class variables for blueprints
 55    brain_blueprint = Brain
 56    input_field_blueprint = InputField
 57    output_field_blueprint = OutputField
 58    column_field_blueprint = ColumnField
 59
 60    def __init__(self, brain: Brain) -> None:
 61        self.logger = get_logger(self)
 62        self._encoder_factory = EncoderFactory()
 63        self._main_brain: Brain = brain
 64        self._brains: list[Brain] = []
 65        self._trainer_input_fields: list[Field] = []
 66        self._trainer_output_fields: list[Field] = []
 67        self._trainer_column_fields: list[Field] = []
 68        self._values: list[Any] = []
 69
 70    @staticmethod
 71    def _encoder_type_from_params(param: ParameterMarker) -> str:
 72        """Map a parameter dataclass to the factory encoder type string."""
 73
 74        encoder_name = param.encoder_class.__name__.lower()
 75        encoder_map = {
 76            "randomdistributedscalarencoder": "rdse",
 77            "dateencoder": "date",
 78            "categoryencoder": "category",
 79            "fourierencoder": "fourier",
 80            "geospatialencoder": "geospatial",
 81            "coordinateencoder": "coordinate",
 82            "scalarencoder": "scalar",
 83        }
 84        if encoder_name not in encoder_map:
 85            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported encoder parameters type: {type(param)}")
 86        return encoder_map[encoder_name]
 87
 88    @property
 89    def brains(self) -> list[Brain]:
 90        """Access the list of cached Brains."""
 91
 92        return self._brains
 93
 94    @property
 95    def main_brain(self) -> Brain:
 96        """Access the main Brain being trained."""
 97        if self._main_brain is None:
 98            raise ValueError(self._BRAIN_NOT_INITIALIZED_ERROR)
 99        return self._main_brain
100
101    @property
102    def input_fields(self) -> list[Field]:
103        """Access the input fields configured for training."""
104        return self._trainer_input_fields
105
106    @property
107    def output_fields(self) -> list[Field]:
108        """Access the output fields configured for training."""
109        return self._trainer_output_fields
110
111    @property
112    def column_fields(self) -> list[Field]:
113        """Access the column fields configured for training."""
114        return self._trainer_column_fields
115
116    @main_brain.setter
117    def main_brain(self, brain: Brain) -> None:
118        """Set the main Brain being trained."""
119        if brain is None:
120            raise ValueError("Cannot set main Brain to None.")
121        elif not isinstance(brain, Brain):
122            raise ValueError("main_brain must be an instance of Brain.")
123
124        self._main_brain = brain
125        if brain not in self._brains:
126            self._brains.append(brain)
127
128    def _setup_io_fields(self, fields: list[tuple[str, int, ParameterMarker]]) -> None:
129        """Setup the fields for the Brain through the passed in tuple.
130
131        Args:
132            fields: A list of tuples containing field name, field size, and encoder parameters.
133
134        Raises:
135            ValueError: If unsupported encoder parameter type is provided.
136
137        Example:
138            fields = [
139                ("consumption_input", 12288, RDSEParameters(size=12288, sparsity=0.02, resolution=0.001, category=False, seed=5)),
140                ("date_input", 12288, DateEncoderParameters(size=12288, resolution=0.001, seed=5)),
141            ]
142
143        Note:
144            Field names ending with '_input' will automatically be treated as InputFields.
145        """
146        for name, size, param in fields:
147            self.logger.info(
148                "Setting up field %-24s size=%5d encoder=%-28s",
149                name,
150                size,
151                type(param).__name__,
152            )
153            encoder_type = self._encoder_type_from_params(param)
154            created_encoder = cast(
155                el.BaseEncoder, self._encoder_factory.create_encoder(encoder_type, asdict(param))
156            )
157            encoder_params = param
158
159            if not isinstance(created_encoder, el.BaseEncoder):
160                self.logger.error("Failed to create encoder for field '%s'", name)
161                continue
162
163            if name.endswith("_input"):
164                field = InputField(size=size, encoder_params=encoder_params)
165
166                field.name = name
167            elif name.endswith("_output"):
168                field = OutputField(size=size, motor_action=(None,))
169                field.name = name
170            else:
171                raise ValueError("Unsupported field type: {}".format(name))
172
173            if isinstance(field, InputField):
174                self._trainer_input_fields.append(field)
175            elif isinstance(field, OutputField):
176                self._trainer_output_fields.append(field)
177
178    def _setup_column_fields(self, num_columns: int, cells_per_column: int) -> None:
179        """Setup the ColumnField for the Brain."""
180        column_field = ColumnField(
181            input_fields=self._trainer_input_fields,
182            non_spatial=True,
183            num_columns=num_columns,
184            cells_per_column=cells_per_column,
185        )
186        column_field.name = "Column_Field"
187        self._trainer_column_fields.append(column_field)
188
189    def _create_brain(self) -> Brain:
190        """Create a Brain instance with the configured fields."""
191
192        if not self._trainer_input_fields:
193            raise ValueError("No input fields defined for the Brain.")
194        if not self._trainer_column_fields:
195            raise ValueError("No column fields defined for the Brain.")
196
197        brain = Brain(
198            {
199                **{field.name: field for field in self._trainer_input_fields if field is not None},
200                **{field.name: field for field in self._trainer_output_fields if field is not None},
201                **{field.name: field for field in self._trainer_column_fields if field is not None},
202            }
203        )
204
205        self.logger.info("Brain created with fields: %s", list(brain.fields.keys()))
206
207        return brain
208
209    def _build_values_list(self, dataset: dict[str, list[Any]]) -> None:
210        """Build a list of values from the dataset for training."""
211
212        for column_name, column_data in dataset.items():
213            self.logger.debug(
214                f"Processing column '{column_name}' with {len(column_data)} data points."
215            )
216            for value in column_data:
217                self._values.append(value)
218        self.logger.info(
219            f"Built values list with {len(self._values)} total data points from dataset."
220        )
221
222    def print_train_stats(
223        self,
224        save_path: str | None = None,
225        test_results: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
226        training_steps: int | None = None,
227    ) -> None:
228        """Print statistics about the training dataset.
229
230        Args:
231            save_path: Optional file path to append the brain report to. If provided,
232                      the report will be appended to the file.
233            test_results: Optional test/prediction results dictionary to include in the report.
234            training_steps: Optional number of training steps to include in the report.
235        """
236        self.logger.info("Training dataset statistics:")
237        self._main_brain.print_stats()
238
239        if save_path:
240            # Create parent directories if they don't exist
241            Path(save_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
242
243            with open(save_path, "a") as f:
244                f.write("\n" + "=" * 80 + "\n")
245                f.write(f"Brain Report - {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}\n")
246                f.write("=" * 80 + "\n")
247
248                # Include training steps if provided
249                if training_steps is not None:
250                    f.write(f"Training Steps: {training_steps}\n")
251                    f.write("-" * 80 + "\n")
252
253                # Capture the brain stats
254
255                old_stdout = sys.stdout
256                sys.stdout = buffer = io.StringIO()
257
258                self._main_brain.print_stats()
259                sys.stdout = old_stdout
260                f.write(buffer.getvalue())
261                f.write("\n")
262
263                # Include test results if provided
264                if test_results:
265                    f.write("\n" + "-" * 80 + "\n")
266                    f.write("PREDICTION TEST RESULTS\n")
267                    f.write("-" * 80 + "\n\n")
268
269                    # Write summary metrics
270                    f.write("Prediction Summary:\n")
271                    f.write(f"  Global MSE: {test_results.get('mean_squared_error', 0.0):.6f}\n")
272                    f.write(
273                        f"  Total prediction failures: {test_results.get('total_prediction_failures', 0)}\n"
274                    )
275                    f.write(
276                        f"  Avg bursting columns: {test_results.get('avg_bursting_columns', 0.0):.3f}\n\n"
277                    )
278
279                    # Write per-field metrics table
280                    if "errors" in test_results and "prediction_failures" in test_results:
281                        f.write("  Per-field metrics:\n")
282                        f.write("    +----------------------------+---------------+-----------+\n")
283                        f.write("    | Field                      |     MSE       | Failures |\n")
284                        f.write("    +----------------------------+---------------+-----------+\n")
285
286                        field_errors = test_results["errors"]
287                        failures = test_results["prediction_failures"]
288
289                        for field_name, errors in field_errors.items():
290                            mse = sum(errors) / len(errors) if errors else 0.0
291                            failure_count = failures.get(field_name, 0)
292                            f.write(
293                                f"    | {field_name:<26} | {mse:>11.6f} | {failure_count:>9} |\n"
294                            )
295
296                        f.write("    +----------------------------+---------------+-----------+\n")
297
298                    f.write("\n")
299
300    def build_brain(self, fields: list[tuple[str, int, el.ParameterMarker]]) -> Brain:
301        """Build the Brain for training. Building the Brain this way allows for more direct control over the fields and their parameters, which can be crucial for effective training.
302
303        Args:
304            fields: A list of tuples containing field name, field size, and encoder parameters.
305
306                Example:
307                fields = [
308                    ("consumption_input", 2048, RDSEParameters(size=2048, sparsity=0.02, resolution=1.0, category=False, seed=5)),
309                    ("date_input", 2048, DateEncoderParameters(size=2048, resolution=1.0, seed=5)),
310                ]
311        Returns:
312            The built Brain instance ready for training.
313
314        """
315        # build a brain based on the provided fields, using the maximum size from the fields to determine the number of columns in the column field and the size of the output SDR. This ensures that the column field can accommodate the largest input field
316
317        size = max(field[1] for field in fields)  # Get the maximum size from the fields
318        # make the input/output fields
319        self._setup_io_fields(fields)
320        # make column fields
321        self._setup_column_fields(num_columns=size, cells_per_column=16)
322        # create the Brain with fields
323        brain = self._create_brain()
324        self._main_brain = brain
325
326        if brain not in self._brains:
327            self._brains.append(brain)
328
329        return brain
330
331    def add_input_field(self, name: str, size: int, encoder_params: el.ParameterMarker) -> None:
332        """Add an input field to the Brain."""
333
334        if self._main_brain is None:
335            raise ValueError(self._BRAIN_NOT_INITIALIZED_ERROR)
336
337        if name.endswith("_input"):
338            field = InputField(size=size, encoder_params=encoder_params)
339            field.name = name
340            self._trainer_input_fields.append(field)
341            self._main_brain.fields[name] = field
342        else:
343            raise ValueError("Input field name must end with '_input'.")
344
345    def add_output_field(self, name: str, size: int, motor_action: tuple[Any, ...]) -> None:
346        """Add an output field to the Brain."""
347
348        if self._main_brain is None:
349            raise ValueError(self._BRAIN_NOT_INITIALIZED_ERROR)
350
351        if name.endswith("_output"):
352            field = OutputField(size=size, motor_action=motor_action)
353            field.name = name
354            self._trainer_output_fields.append(field)
355            self._main_brain.fields[name] = field
356        else:
357            raise ValueError("Output field name must end with '_output'.")
358
359    def add_column_field(self, name: str, num_columns: int, cells_per_column: int) -> None:
360        """Add a column field to the Brain."""
361
362        if self._main_brain is None:
363            raise ValueError(self._BRAIN_NOT_INITIALIZED_ERROR)
364
365        if name.endswith("_column"):
366            field = ColumnField(
367                input_fields=self._trainer_input_fields,
368                non_spatial=True,
369                num_columns=num_columns,
370                cells_per_column=cells_per_column,
371            )
372            field.name = name
373            self._trainer_column_fields.append(field)
374            self._main_brain.fields[name] = field
375        else:
376            raise ValueError("Column field name must end with '_column'.")
377
378    def train_column(self, brain: Brain, column: dict[str, list[Any]], steps: int) -> None:
379        """Train the Brain on the specified dataset."""
380
381        self._main_brain = brain if brain is not None else self._main_brain
382
383        if len(column.keys()) == 0:
384            raise ValueError("Dataset is empty. Cannot train on an empty dataset.")
385        elif len(column.keys()) > 1:
386            raise ValueError(
387                "Dataset has more than one column. Use train_brain() to train on multiple columns."
388            )
389        else:
390
391            self.logger.info(f"Training one column with {steps} data points.")
392
393        for step in range(steps):
394            # Get the current data point from the column
395            name = list(column.keys())[0]
396            value = column[name][step % len(column[name])]
397            self.logger.info(f"Step {step + 1}/{steps}, Data Point: {name}={value}")
398
399            for field in self._trainer_input_fields:
400                if field.name == name:
401                    input_dict = {field.name: value}
402                    break
403            else:
404                raise ValueError(f"No matching input field found for column '{name}'.")
405
406            # Step the Brain with the prepared inputs
407            brain.step(inputs=input_dict, learn=True)
408
409    def train_full_brain(self, brain: Brain, dataset: dict[str, list[Any]], steps: int) -> None:
410        """Train the Brain on the specified dataset."""
411
412        self._main_brain = brain if brain is not None else self._main_brain
413
414        self.logger.info(f"Training on dataset with columns: {list(dataset.keys())}")
415
416        for column_name, column_data in dataset.items():
417            self.logger.debug(f"Column '{column_name}' has {len(column_data)} data points.")
418
419        for step in range(steps):
420            # Get the current data point from the dataset
421            column_data = {key: dataset[key][step % len(dataset[key])] for key in dataset}
422            # avoid index out of range by looping over dataset if steps exceed its size
423            self.logger.debug(f"Step {step + 1}/{steps}, Data Point: {column_data}")
424
425            # Prepare the input dictionary for the Brain
426            input_dict = {}
427            for field in self._trainer_input_fields:
428                # Check if the field name exists in the data point and add it to the input dictionary
429                if field.name in column_data:
430                    input_dict[field.name] = column_data[field.name]
431                else:
432                    raise ValueError(f"Data point is missing required input field: {field.name}")
433
434            # Step the Brain with the prepared inputs
435            brain.step(inputs=input_dict, learn=True)
436
437    def test(
438        self, brain: Brain | None, dataset: dict[str, list[Any]], steps: int | None = None
439    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
440        """Replay inputs without learning and report prediction accuracy metrics."""
441
442        if brain is None:
443            brain = self._main_brain
444        if brain is None:
445            raise ValueError(self._BRAIN_NOT_INITIALIZED_ERROR)
446
447        if not isinstance(dataset, dict) or not dataset:
448            raise ValueError("Dataset must be a non-empty dict mapping field names to data.")
449
450        steps = steps or min(len(values) for values in dataset.values())
451        field_errors: dict[str, list[float]] = {name: [] for name in dataset}
452        prediction_failures: dict[str, int] = dict.fromkeys(dataset, 0)
453        evaluation_bursts: list[int] = []
454        missing_prediction_penalty = 1.0
455
456        self.logger.info(
457            "Testing predictions for %d steps on fields: %s",
458            steps,
459            list(dataset.keys()),
460        )
461
462        for step in range(steps):
463            predictions = brain.prediction()
464
465            for field_name, series in dataset.items():
466                expected_value = series[step % len(series)]
467                predicted_value = (
468                    predictions.get(field_name) if isinstance(predictions, dict) else None
469                )
470                if predicted_value is None:
471                    prediction_failures[field_name] += 1
472                    field_errors[field_name].append(missing_prediction_penalty)
473                    continue
474
475                try:
476                    diff = float(expected_value) - float(predicted_value)
477                    field_errors[field_name].append(diff * diff)
478                except (TypeError, ValueError):
479                    field_errors[field_name].append(
480                        0.0 if expected_value == predicted_value else missing_prediction_penalty
481                    )
482
483            inputs = {name: series[step % len(series)] for name, series in dataset.items()}
484            brain.step(inputs, learn=False)
485
486            bursts = sum(len(column_field.bursting_columns) for column_field in brain.column_fields)
487            evaluation_bursts.append(bursts)
488
489        flattened_errors = [err for errors in field_errors.values() for err in errors]
490        mean_squared_error = (
491            sum(flattened_errors) / len(flattened_errors) if flattened_errors else 0.0
492        )
493        avg_bursting_columns = (
494            sum(evaluation_bursts) / len(evaluation_bursts) if evaluation_bursts else 0.0
495        )
496        total_failures = sum(prediction_failures.values())
497
498        field_rows = []
499        for field_name, errors in field_errors.items():
500            mse = sum(errors) / len(errors) if errors else 0.0
501            failures = prediction_failures[field_name]
502            field_rows.append((field_name, mse, failures))
503
504        table_lines = [
505            "+----------------------------+---------------+-----------+",
506            "| Field                      |     MSE       | Failures |",
507            "+----------------------------+---------------+-----------+",
508        ]
509        for name, mse, failures in field_rows:
510            table_lines.append(f"| {name:<26} | {mse:>11.6f} | {failures:>9} |")
511        table_lines.append("+----------------------------+---------------+-----------+")
512
513        summary_lines = [
514            "Prediction Summary:",
515            f"  Global MSE: {mean_squared_error:.6f}",
516            f"  Total prediction failures: {total_failures}",
517            f"  Avg bursting columns: {avg_bursting_columns:.3f}",
518            "  Per-field metrics:",
519        ]
520        summary_lines.extend(f"    {line}" for line in table_lines)
521
522        self.logger.info("\n".join(summary_lines))
523
524        return {
525            "mean_squared_error": mean_squared_error,
526            "prediction_failures": prediction_failures,
527            "total_prediction_failures": total_failures,
528            "avg_bursting_columns": avg_bursting_columns,
529            "evaluation_bursts": evaluation_bursts,
530            "errors": field_errors,
531        }
532
533    def build_full_brain(
534        self,
535        dataset: dict[Any, list[Any]],
536        size: int = 2048,
537        params: el.ParameterMarker | None = None,
538    ) -> Brain:
539        """Build a full Brain with all fields based on the dataset.
540
541        This method automatically determines the appropriate encoder type for each field
542        based on the data type of the values in the dataset.
543
544        Args:
545            dataset: A dictionary mapping field names to lists of data points.
546            size: The size of the input and column fields. Defaults to 2048.
547            params: Optional encoder parameters to use for all fields. If not provided,
548                default parameters will be used based on field type.
549
550        Returns:
551            A fully built Brain instance with configured input, column, and output fields.
552
553        Raises:
554            ValueError: If dataset contains unsupported data types.
555
556        Example:
557            brain = trainer.build_full_brain(
558                dataset={
559                    "temperature": [20.5, 21.0, 19.8, ...],
560                    "humidity": [0.45, 0.50, 0.40, ...],
561                    "date": [datetime(2023, 1, 1), datetime(2023, 1, 2), datetime(2023, 1, 3), ...],
562                }, size=2048, params=RDSEParameters(size=2048, sparsity=0.02, resolution=0.01, category=False, seed=5)
563            )
564
565        This method automatically determines the appropriate encoder type for each field based on the data type of the values in the dataset. It then builds the Brain with input fields for each column, a column field that connects to all input fields, and output fields as needed.
566
567        # TODO: add a dict of ParameterMarker-compatible configs to specify encoder parameters for each field when building the brain, and use those parameters to build the brain with the appropriate encoders for each field type. This allows for more flexible and customized brain building based on the dataset characteristics.
568
569        Mapping of data types to encoder parameters:
570        - Numerical (int, float): el.RDSEParameters
571        - Categorical (str): el.CategoryParameters
572        - Date/Time (datetime): el.DateEncoderParameters
573        - Spatial (list, np.ndarray): el.FourierEncoderParameters
574        - Tuple of (numerical, numerical): el.GeospatialParameters
575        - Other types will raise a ValueError indicating unsupported data type.
576
577        """
578
579        for key, values in dataset.items():
580            if isinstance(values[0], (int, float)):
581                encoder_params = (
582                    el.RDSEParameters(
583                        size=params.size,
584                        sparsity=params.sparsity,
585                        resolution=params.resolution,
586                        category=False,
587                        seed=params.seed,
588                    )
589                    if isinstance(params, el.RDSEParameters)
590                    else el.RDSEParameters(size=size)
591                )
592            elif isinstance(values[0], str):
593                encoder_params = (
594                    el.CategoryParameters(
595                        size=params.size,
596                        category_list=list(set(values)),
597                        rdse_used=params.rdse_used,
598                    )
599                    if isinstance(params, el.CategoryParameters)
600                    else el.CategoryParameters(
601                        size=size, category_list=list(set(values)), rdse_used=False
602                    )
603                )
604            elif isinstance(values[0], (list, np.ndarray)):
605                encoder_params = (
606                    el.FourierEncoderParameters(
607                        size=params.size,
608                        frequency_ranges=params.frequency_ranges,
609                        sparsity_in_ranges=params.sparsity_in_ranges,
610                        resolutions_in_ranges=params.resolutions_in_ranges,
611                        seed=params.seed,
612                    )
613                    if isinstance(params, el.FourierEncoderParameters)
614                    else el.FourierEncoderParameters(size=size)
615                )
616            elif isinstance(values[0], datetime):
617                encoder_params = (
618                    el.DateEncoderParameters(
619                        size=params.size,
620                        season_active_bits=params.season_active_bits,
621                        season_radius=params.season_radius,
622                        day_of_week_active_bits=params.day_of_week_active_bits,
623                        day_of_week_radius=params.day_of_week_radius,
624                        weekend_active_bits=params.weekend_active_bits,
625                        holiday_active_bits=params.holiday_active_bits,
626                        holiday_dates=params.holiday_dates,
627                        time_of_day_active_bits=params.time_of_day_active_bits,
628                        time_of_day_radius=params.time_of_day_radius,
629                        custom_active_bits=params.custom_active_bits,
630                        custom_days=params.custom_days,
631                        rdse_used=params.rdse_used,
632                    )
633                    if isinstance(params, el.DateEncoderParameters)
634                    else el.DateEncoderParameters(size=size)
635                )
636            elif (
637                isinstance(values[0], tuple)
638                and len(values[0]) >= 2
639                and all(isinstance(v, (int, float)) for v in values[0])
640            ):
641                encoder_params = (
642                    el.GeospatialParameters(
643                        size=params.size,
644                        max_radius=params.max_radius,
645                        scale=params.scale,
646                        timestep=params.timestep,
647                        use_altitude=params.use_altitude,
648                    )
649                    if isinstance(params, el.GeospatialParameters)
650                    else el.GeospatialParameters(size=size)
651                )
652            else:
653                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported data type for field '{key}': {type(values[0])}")
654
655            self._setup_io_fields([(f"{key}_input", size, encoder_params)])
656
657        self._setup_column_fields(num_columns=size, cells_per_column=32)
658
659        brain = self._create_brain()
660
661        self._main_brain = brain
662        self._brains.append(brain)
663
664        return brain
665
666    def show_active_columns(self, brain: Brain, dataset_name: str | None = None) -> None:
667        """Show the active columns in the Brain."""
668
669        grapher.show_active_columns(brain, dataset_name)
670
671    def show_heat_map(self, brain: Brain, dataset_name: str | None = None) -> None:
672        """Show a heat map of the Brain's column duty cycle activity."""
673
674        grapher.show_heat_map(brain, dataset_name)
675
676    # TODO: Implement save_brain and load_brain methods for persistence of trained Brains
677    def save_brain(self, brain: Brain, filename: str) -> None:
678        """Save the Brain to a file."""
679        # Implement saving logic, e.g., using joblib or pickle
680
681    def load_brain(self, filename: str) -> Brain:
682        """Load a Brain from a file."""
683        # Implement loading logic, e.g., using joblib or pickle
684        return Brain()  # Placeholder return

Build a Trainer for training Brains on a dataset.

Args: brain: Brain instance to be trained with this trainer.

Attributes: brain_blueprint: Blueprint class used for creating Brain instances. input_field_blueprint: Blueprint class used for creating input fields. output_field_blueprint: Blueprint class used for creating output fields. column_field_blueprint: Blueprint class used for creating column fields.

Trainer(brain: Brain) 
60    def __init__(self, brain: Brain) -> None:
61        self.logger = get_logger(self)
62        self._encoder_factory = EncoderFactory()
63        self._main_brain: Brain = brain
64        self._brains: list[Brain] = []
65        self._trainer_input_fields: list[Field] = []
66        self._trainer_output_fields: list[Field] = []
67        self._trainer_column_fields: list[Field] = []
68        self._values: list[Any] = []
brain_blueprint = <class 'Brain'>
input_field_blueprint = <class 'InputField'>
output_field_blueprint = <class 'OutputField'>
column_field_blueprint = <class 'ColumnField'>
logger
brains: list[Brain] 
88    @property
89    def brains(self) -> list[Brain]:
90        """Access the list of cached Brains."""
91
92        return self._brains

Access the list of cached Brains.

main_brain: Brain 
94    @property
95    def main_brain(self) -> Brain:
96        """Access the main Brain being trained."""
97        if self._main_brain is None:
98            raise ValueError(self._BRAIN_NOT_INITIALIZED_ERROR)
99        return self._main_brain

Access the main Brain being trained.

input_fields: list[Field] 
101    @property
102    def input_fields(self) -> list[Field]:
103        """Access the input fields configured for training."""
104        return self._trainer_input_fields

Access the input fields configured for training.

output_fields: list[Field] 
106    @property
107    def output_fields(self) -> list[Field]:
108        """Access the output fields configured for training."""
109        return self._trainer_output_fields

Access the output fields configured for training.

column_fields: list[Field] 
111    @property
112    def column_fields(self) -> list[Field]:
113        """Access the column fields configured for training."""
114        return self._trainer_column_fields

Access the column fields configured for training.

def print_train_stats( self, save_path: str | None = None, test_results: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None, training_steps: int | None = None) -> None: 
222    def print_train_stats(
223        self,
224        save_path: str | None = None,
225        test_results: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
226        training_steps: int | None = None,
227    ) -> None:
228        """Print statistics about the training dataset.
229
230        Args:
231            save_path: Optional file path to append the brain report to. If provided,
232                      the report will be appended to the file.
233            test_results: Optional test/prediction results dictionary to include in the report.
234            training_steps: Optional number of training steps to include in the report.
235        """
236        self.logger.info("Training dataset statistics:")
237        self._main_brain.print_stats()
238
239        if save_path:
240            # Create parent directories if they don't exist
241            Path(save_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
242
243            with open(save_path, "a") as f:
244                f.write("\n" + "=" * 80 + "\n")
245                f.write(f"Brain Report - {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}\n")
246                f.write("=" * 80 + "\n")
247
248                # Include training steps if provided
249                if training_steps is not None:
250                    f.write(f"Training Steps: {training_steps}\n")
251                    f.write("-" * 80 + "\n")
252
253                # Capture the brain stats
254
255                old_stdout = sys.stdout
256                sys.stdout = buffer = io.StringIO()
257
258                self._main_brain.print_stats()
259                sys.stdout = old_stdout
260                f.write(buffer.getvalue())
261                f.write("\n")
262
263                # Include test results if provided
264                if test_results:
265                    f.write("\n" + "-" * 80 + "\n")
266                    f.write("PREDICTION TEST RESULTS\n")
267                    f.write("-" * 80 + "\n\n")
268
269                    # Write summary metrics
270                    f.write("Prediction Summary:\n")
271                    f.write(f"  Global MSE: {test_results.get('mean_squared_error', 0.0):.6f}\n")
272                    f.write(
273                        f"  Total prediction failures: {test_results.get('total_prediction_failures', 0)}\n"
274                    )
275                    f.write(
276                        f"  Avg bursting columns: {test_results.get('avg_bursting_columns', 0.0):.3f}\n\n"
277                    )
278
279                    # Write per-field metrics table
280                    if "errors" in test_results and "prediction_failures" in test_results:
281                        f.write("  Per-field metrics:\n")
282                        f.write("    +----------------------------+---------------+-----------+\n")
283                        f.write("    | Field                      |     MSE       | Failures |\n")
284                        f.write("    +----------------------------+---------------+-----------+\n")
285
286                        field_errors = test_results["errors"]
287                        failures = test_results["prediction_failures"]
288
289                        for field_name, errors in field_errors.items():
290                            mse = sum(errors) / len(errors) if errors else 0.0
291                            failure_count = failures.get(field_name, 0)
292                            f.write(
293                                f"    | {field_name:<26} | {mse:>11.6f} | {failure_count:>9} |\n"
294                            )
295
296                        f.write("    +----------------------------+---------------+-----------+\n")
297
298                    f.write("\n")

Print statistics about the training dataset.

Args: save_path: Optional file path to append the brain report to. If provided, the report will be appended to the file. test_results: Optional test/prediction results dictionary to include in the report. training_steps: Optional number of training steps to include in the report.

def build_brain( self, fields: list[tuple[str, int, psu_capstone.encoder_layer.ParameterMarker]]) -> Brain: 
300    def build_brain(self, fields: list[tuple[str, int, el.ParameterMarker]]) -> Brain:
301        """Build the Brain for training. Building the Brain this way allows for more direct control over the fields and their parameters, which can be crucial for effective training.
302
303        Args:
304            fields: A list of tuples containing field name, field size, and encoder parameters.
305
306                Example:
307                fields = [
308                    ("consumption_input", 2048, RDSEParameters(size=2048, sparsity=0.02, resolution=1.0, category=False, seed=5)),
309                    ("date_input", 2048, DateEncoderParameters(size=2048, resolution=1.0, seed=5)),
310                ]
311        Returns:
312            The built Brain instance ready for training.
313
314        """
315        # build a brain based on the provided fields, using the maximum size from the fields to determine the number of columns in the column field and the size of the output SDR. This ensures that the column field can accommodate the largest input field
316
317        size = max(field[1] for field in fields)  # Get the maximum size from the fields
318        # make the input/output fields
319        self._setup_io_fields(fields)
320        # make column fields
321        self._setup_column_fields(num_columns=size, cells_per_column=16)
322        # create the Brain with fields
323        brain = self._create_brain()
324        self._main_brain = brain
325
326        if brain not in self._brains:
327            self._brains.append(brain)
328
329        return brain

Build the Brain for training. Building the Brain this way allows for more direct control over the fields and their parameters, which can be crucial for effective training.

Args: fields: A list of tuples containing field name, field size, and encoder parameters.

    Example:
    fields = [
        ("consumption_input", 2048, RDSEParameters(size=2048, sparsity=0.02, resolution=1.0, category=False, seed=5)),
        ("date_input", 2048, DateEncoderParameters(size=2048, resolution=1.0, seed=5)),
    ]

Returns: The built Brain instance ready for training.

def add_input_field( self, name: str, size: int, encoder_params: psu_capstone.encoder_layer.ParameterMarker) -> None: 
331    def add_input_field(self, name: str, size: int, encoder_params: el.ParameterMarker) -> None:
332        """Add an input field to the Brain."""
333
334        if self._main_brain is None:
335            raise ValueError(self._BRAIN_NOT_INITIALIZED_ERROR)
336
337        if name.endswith("_input"):
338            field = InputField(size=size, encoder_params=encoder_params)
339            field.name = name
340            self._trainer_input_fields.append(field)
341            self._main_brain.fields[name] = field
342        else:
343            raise ValueError("Input field name must end with '_input'.")

Add an input field to the Brain.

def add_output_field(self, name: str, size: int, motor_action: tuple[typing.Any, ...]) -> None: 
345    def add_output_field(self, name: str, size: int, motor_action: tuple[Any, ...]) -> None:
346        """Add an output field to the Brain."""
347
348        if self._main_brain is None:
349            raise ValueError(self._BRAIN_NOT_INITIALIZED_ERROR)
350
351        if name.endswith("_output"):
352            field = OutputField(size=size, motor_action=motor_action)
353            field.name = name
354            self._trainer_output_fields.append(field)
355            self._main_brain.fields[name] = field
356        else:
357            raise ValueError("Output field name must end with '_output'.")

Add an output field to the Brain.

def add_column_field(self, name: str, num_columns: int, cells_per_column: int) -> None: 
359    def add_column_field(self, name: str, num_columns: int, cells_per_column: int) -> None:
360        """Add a column field to the Brain."""
361
362        if self._main_brain is None:
363            raise ValueError(self._BRAIN_NOT_INITIALIZED_ERROR)
364
365        if name.endswith("_column"):
366            field = ColumnField(
367                input_fields=self._trainer_input_fields,
368                non_spatial=True,
369                num_columns=num_columns,
370                cells_per_column=cells_per_column,
371            )
372            field.name = name
373            self._trainer_column_fields.append(field)
374            self._main_brain.fields[name] = field
375        else:
376            raise ValueError("Column field name must end with '_column'.")

Add a column field to the Brain.

def train_column( self, brain: Brain, column: dict[str, list[typing.Any]], steps: int) -> None: 
378    def train_column(self, brain: Brain, column: dict[str, list[Any]], steps: int) -> None:
379        """Train the Brain on the specified dataset."""
380
381        self._main_brain = brain if brain is not None else self._main_brain
382
383        if len(column.keys()) == 0:
384            raise ValueError("Dataset is empty. Cannot train on an empty dataset.")
385        elif len(column.keys()) > 1:
386            raise ValueError(
387                "Dataset has more than one column. Use train_brain() to train on multiple columns."
388            )
389        else:
390
391            self.logger.info(f"Training one column with {steps} data points.")
392
393        for step in range(steps):
394            # Get the current data point from the column
395            name = list(column.keys())[0]
396            value = column[name][step % len(column[name])]
397            self.logger.info(f"Step {step + 1}/{steps}, Data Point: {name}={value}")
398
399            for field in self._trainer_input_fields:
400                if field.name == name:
401                    input_dict = {field.name: value}
402                    break
403            else:
404                raise ValueError(f"No matching input field found for column '{name}'.")
405
406            # Step the Brain with the prepared inputs
407            brain.step(inputs=input_dict, learn=True)

Train the Brain on the specified dataset.

def train_full_brain( self, brain: Brain, dataset: dict[str, list[typing.Any]], steps: int) -> None: 
409    def train_full_brain(self, brain: Brain, dataset: dict[str, list[Any]], steps: int) -> None:
410        """Train the Brain on the specified dataset."""
411
412        self._main_brain = brain if brain is not None else self._main_brain
413
414        self.logger.info(f"Training on dataset with columns: {list(dataset.keys())}")
415
416        for column_name, column_data in dataset.items():
417            self.logger.debug(f"Column '{column_name}' has {len(column_data)} data points.")
418
419        for step in range(steps):
420            # Get the current data point from the dataset
421            column_data = {key: dataset[key][step % len(dataset[key])] for key in dataset}
422            # avoid index out of range by looping over dataset if steps exceed its size
423            self.logger.debug(f"Step {step + 1}/{steps}, Data Point: {column_data}")
424
425            # Prepare the input dictionary for the Brain
426            input_dict = {}
427            for field in self._trainer_input_fields:
428                # Check if the field name exists in the data point and add it to the input dictionary
429                if field.name in column_data:
430                    input_dict[field.name] = column_data[field.name]
431                else:
432                    raise ValueError(f"Data point is missing required input field: {field.name}")
433
434            # Step the Brain with the prepared inputs
435            brain.step(inputs=input_dict, learn=True)

Train the Brain on the specified dataset.

def test( self, brain: Brain | None, dataset: dict[str, list[typing.Any]], steps: int | None = None) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
437    def test(
438        self, brain: Brain | None, dataset: dict[str, list[Any]], steps: int | None = None
439    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
440        """Replay inputs without learning and report prediction accuracy metrics."""
441
442        if brain is None:
443            brain = self._main_brain
444        if brain is None:
445            raise ValueError(self._BRAIN_NOT_INITIALIZED_ERROR)
446
447        if not isinstance(dataset, dict) or not dataset:
448            raise ValueError("Dataset must be a non-empty dict mapping field names to data.")
449
450        steps = steps or min(len(values) for values in dataset.values())
451        field_errors: dict[str, list[float]] = {name: [] for name in dataset}
452        prediction_failures: dict[str, int] = dict.fromkeys(dataset, 0)
453        evaluation_bursts: list[int] = []
454        missing_prediction_penalty = 1.0
455
456        self.logger.info(
457            "Testing predictions for %d steps on fields: %s",
458            steps,
459            list(dataset.keys()),
460        )
461
462        for step in range(steps):
463            predictions = brain.prediction()
464
465            for field_name, series in dataset.items():
466                expected_value = series[step % len(series)]
467                predicted_value = (
468                    predictions.get(field_name) if isinstance(predictions, dict) else None
469                )
470                if predicted_value is None:
471                    prediction_failures[field_name] += 1
472                    field_errors[field_name].append(missing_prediction_penalty)
473                    continue
474
475                try:
476                    diff = float(expected_value) - float(predicted_value)
477                    field_errors[field_name].append(diff * diff)
478                except (TypeError, ValueError):
479                    field_errors[field_name].append(
480                        0.0 if expected_value == predicted_value else missing_prediction_penalty
481                    )
482
483            inputs = {name: series[step % len(series)] for name, series in dataset.items()}
484            brain.step(inputs, learn=False)
485
486            bursts = sum(len(column_field.bursting_columns) for column_field in brain.column_fields)
487            evaluation_bursts.append(bursts)
488
489        flattened_errors = [err for errors in field_errors.values() for err in errors]
490        mean_squared_error = (
491            sum(flattened_errors) / len(flattened_errors) if flattened_errors else 0.0
492        )
493        avg_bursting_columns = (
494            sum(evaluation_bursts) / len(evaluation_bursts) if evaluation_bursts else 0.0
495        )
496        total_failures = sum(prediction_failures.values())
497
498        field_rows = []
499        for field_name, errors in field_errors.items():
500            mse = sum(errors) / len(errors) if errors else 0.0
501            failures = prediction_failures[field_name]
502            field_rows.append((field_name, mse, failures))
503
504        table_lines = [
505            "+----------------------------+---------------+-----------+",
506            "| Field                      |     MSE       | Failures |",
507            "+----------------------------+---------------+-----------+",
508        ]
509        for name, mse, failures in field_rows:
510            table_lines.append(f"| {name:<26} | {mse:>11.6f} | {failures:>9} |")
